A: Agilent Technologies provides analytical instruments, software, services, and consumables for laboratories worldwide. Its product portfolio includes liquid chromatography, mass spectrometry, and genomics solutions used in life sciences, diagnostics, and chemical analysis. The company serves pharmaceutical, environmental, food safety, and academic research markets.
AAPL: Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones (iPhone), personal computers (Mac), tablets (iPad), wearables (Apple Watch, AirPods), and services (App Store, Apple Music, iCloud, Apple TV+). It is the world's most valuable public company by market capitalization and operates a tightly integrated hardware-software ecosystem that drives high customer retention.
ABBV: AbbVie is a global biopharmaceutical company focused on immunology, oncology, neuroscience, and eye care. Its flagship product Humira was the world's best-selling drug for nearly two decades; the company has since transitioned growth to Skyrizi and Rinvoq in immunology and expanded its oncology portfolio through the Allergan acquisition.
ABNB: Airbnb operates a global online marketplace connecting travelers with hosts who offer unique accommodations and experiences. The platform spans over 220 countries and regions with millions of active listings, ranging from apartments to treehouses. Airbnb pioneered the modern home-sharing economy and generates revenue primarily through service fees on bookings.
ABT: Abbott Laboratories is a diversified healthcare company with four business segments: diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional products, and established pharmaceuticals. Its products include the FreeStyle Libre continuous glucose monitoring system, infant formula brands like Similac, and a broad range of diagnostic testing instruments used by hospitals and labs worldwide.
ACGL: Arch Capital Group is a global insurance, reinsurance, and mortgage insurance company based in Bermuda. The company underwrites specialty lines of property and casualty insurance through its insurance and reinsurance segments, serving clients worldwide with a focus on disciplined underwriting and risk selection.
ACN: Accenture is a global professional services company providing strategy, consulting, digital transformation, technology, and operations services. It serves clients across all major industries and is one of the world's largest IT services firms by revenue. Accenture employs over 700,000 people across more than 120 countries.
ADBE: Adobe is a global software company known for its Creative Cloud suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro), Document Cloud (Acrobat, PDF), and Experience Cloud (digital marketing and analytics). Adobe pioneered the shift to subscription-based software and dominates the creative professional tools market.
ADI: Analog Devices designs and manufactures analog, mixed-signal, and digital signal processing integrated circuits. Its products convert real-world signals (temperature, pressure, sound, light) into digital data for processing, serving the industrial, automotive, communications, and consumer electronics markets.
ADM: Archer-Daniels-Midland is one of the world's largest agricultural processors and food ingredient providers. The company procures, transports, stores, processes, and merchandises agricultural commodities including oilseeds, corn, wheat, and cocoa, transforming them into food ingredients, animal feed, and renewable fuels.
ADP: Automatic Data Processing is one of the world's largest providers of human capital management solutions, including payroll processing, benefits administration, talent management, and HR outsourcing. ADP processes payroll for approximately one in six U.S. workers and serves over 900,000 clients globally.
ADSK: Autodesk develops 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software used by architects, engineers, designers, and digital artists. Its flagship products include AutoCAD, Revit, Fusion 360, and Maya, enabling building information modeling, product design, manufacturing, and visual effects across the architecture, engineering, construction, and media industries.
AEE: Ameren Corporation is a regulated electric and natural gas utility holding company serving approximately 2.4 million electric and 900,000 natural gas customers in Missouri and Illinois. The company generates, transmits, and distributes electricity and distributes natural gas through its Ameren Missouri and Ameren Illinois subsidiaries.
AEP: American Electric Power is one of the largest electric utilities in the United States, delivering electricity to over 5.5 million customers across 11 states. The company operates a diverse generation fleet and owns the nation's largest electricity transmission system spanning nearly 40,000 miles.
AES: The AES Corporation is a global energy company that owns and operates a diversified portfolio of power generation and utility businesses across 15 countries. The company has been aggressively transitioning toward renewable energy, with a growing pipeline of solar, wind, and energy storage projects.
AFL: Aflac is the world's largest provider of supplemental health and life insurance, best known for its cancer, accident, and critical illness policies that pay cash benefits directly to policyholders. The company operates primarily in the United States and Japan, where it insures one in four households.
AIG: American International Group is a global insurance company offering a range of property casualty, life, and retirement products. Once the world's largest insurer, AIG underwent a major restructuring after the 2008 financial crisis and has since streamlined its operations to focus on core commercial and personal insurance lines.
AIZ: Assurant is a global provider of specialty insurance products and related services, focusing on housing and lifestyle protection. Its products include renters insurance, lender-placed homeowners insurance, and device protection programs for mobile phones and consumer electronics through partnerships with major carriers and retailers.
AJG: Arthur J. Gallagher is one of the world's largest insurance brokerage, risk management, and consulting firms. The company serves commercial, industrial, institutional, and governmental organizations by placing insurance and reinsurance and providing risk management and claims services.
AKAM: Akamai Technologies operates one of the world's largest content delivery networks (CDN) and cloud security platforms. The company helps businesses deliver fast, reliable, and secure digital experiences by distributing content from servers positioned close to end users across thousands of locations globally.
ALB: Albemarle Corporation is the world's largest producer of lithium, a critical material for electric vehicle batteries and energy storage systems. The company also produces bromine-based specialty chemicals used in flame retardants and catalysts for petroleum refining.
ALGN: Align Technology is the maker of Invisalign clear aligners, the world's most widely used clear aligner system for orthodontic treatment. The company also manufactures iTero intraoral scanners used by dental professionals for digital impressions and treatment planning.
ALL: The Allstate Corporation is one of the largest publicly held personal lines property and casualty insurers in the United States. The company provides auto, home, life, and other insurance products through a network of exclusive agents, independent agents, and direct-to-consumer channels.
AMAT: Applied Materials is the world's largest supplier of semiconductor manufacturing equipment, providing the machines, services, and software that chipmakers use to produce advanced integrated circuits. The company's products are essential to fabricating virtually every new chip in the world.
AMCR: Amcor is a global leader in responsible packaging solutions, producing flexible and rigid packaging for food, beverage, pharmaceutical, medical, home and personal care, and other consumer products. The company operates approximately 200 sites across 40+ countries and is focused on developing recyclable and sustainable packaging.
AMD: Advanced Micro Devices designs and sells high-performance computing and graphics processors, including CPUs (Ryzen, EPYC), GPUs (Radeon, Instinct), and adaptive computing products (FPGAs via Xilinx). AMD competes directly with Intel in data center and PC processors and with NVIDIA in GPU computing and AI accelerators.
AME: AMETEK is a global manufacturer of electronic instruments and electromechanical devices. The company serves diversified end markets including aerospace and defense, medical, industrial automation, and power through its Electronic Instruments Group and Electromechanical Group segments.
AMGN: Amgen is one of the world's largest biotechnology companies, pioneering treatments in oncology, cardiovascular disease, bone health, nephrology, and inflammation. Key products include Enbrel, Prolia, Repatha, and the biosimilar portfolio. Amgen was among the first companies to develop biologic medicines at scale.
AMP: Ameriprise Financial is a diversified financial services company providing financial planning, asset management, insurance, and annuity products to individual and institutional clients. The company operates through its Advice & Wealth Management, Asset Management, and Retirement & Protection Solutions segments.
AMT: American Tower Corporation is one of the largest global real estate investment trusts and a leading independent owner and operator of wireless communications infrastructure. The company owns and operates approximately 225,000 cell towers and data center facilities across 25 countries.
AMZN: Amazon is the world's largest e-commerce company and a dominant force in cloud computing through Amazon Web Services (AWS). The company also operates in digital advertising, streaming media (Prime Video), smart devices (Alexa, Echo), and grocery retail (Whole Foods). AWS is the world's leading cloud infrastructure provider.
ANET: Arista Networks designs and sells high-performance cloud networking solutions, including switches, routers, and network management software. The company's products power the data centers of major cloud providers, financial institutions, and enterprises, with a focus on ultra-low latency and software-defined networking.
ANSS: ANSYS develops and markets engineering simulation software used to predict how products will behave in real-world environments. Its tools simulate structural mechanics, fluid dynamics, electromagnetics, and semiconductor behavior, serving industries from aerospace and automotive to electronics and healthcare.
AON: Aon plc is a global professional services firm providing risk management, insurance and reinsurance brokerage, and human capital consulting. The company helps organizations assess and manage risks, provides employee benefits solutions, and offers data analytics for insurance and risk markets.
APD: Air Products and Chemicals is one of the world's largest industrial gas companies, supplying atmospheric gases (oxygen, nitrogen, argon), process gases (hydrogen, helium), and related equipment to customers in the energy, healthcare, food, electronics, and chemical industries.
APH: Amphenol Corporation is a leading global manufacturer of interconnect solutions, including electrical, electronic, and fiber-optic connectors, cable assemblies, and sensors. The company serves virtually every major electronics market, from data communications and mobile devices to military, aerospace, and automotive applications.
APO: Apollo Global Management is a leading global alternative investment manager with approximately $700 billion in assets under management across credit, private equity, and real assets strategies. The company also operates Athene, a retirement services platform providing annuities and funding agreements.
APP: AppLovin is a technology company that provides software solutions for mobile app developers to grow their businesses. Its platform includes AI-powered advertising technology, monetization tools, and a portfolio of mobile gaming studios. The company has expanded into connected TV advertising and e-commerce.
APTV: Aptiv is a global technology company focused on developing safer, greener, and more connected solutions for the automotive industry. The company provides advanced electrical architecture, active safety systems, and autonomous driving technologies to major automakers worldwide.
ARE: Alexandria Real Estate Equities is a REIT focused on life science, agri-food tech, and technology campuses in innovation clusters. The company develops and operates Class A collaborative research and office spaces in key markets including Boston, San Francisco, San Diego, and Research Triangle.
ARES: Ares Management Corporation is a global alternative investment manager with approximately $450 billion in assets under management across credit, private equity, real estate, and infrastructure strategies. The company provides flexible capital solutions to companies and serves institutional and retail investors worldwide.
ARM: Arm Holdings designs and licenses the ARM architecture, the world's most widely used processor architecture, powering virtually all smartphones and an increasing share of laptops, servers, and IoT devices. Rather than manufacturing chips, Arm licenses its designs to semiconductor companies like Qualcomm, Apple, and Samsung.
ATO: Atmos Energy Corporation is one of the largest natural-gas-only distributors in the United States, serving approximately 3.3 million customers across eight states. The company also operates regulated natural gas transmission and storage assets in Texas, Louisiana, and Mississippi.
AVB: AvalonBay Communities is a REIT that develops, acquires, and manages high-quality apartment communities in leading metropolitan areas across the United States. The company focuses on supply-constrained coastal markets and operates a portfolio of approximately 90,000 apartment homes.
AVGO: Broadcom is a global technology leader that designs, develops, and supplies semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions. Its products include networking chips, broadband, storage, wireless connectivity, and enterprise software (VMware, CA Technologies, Symantec). Broadcom's chips are used in data centers, smartphones, and industrial equipment.
AWK: American Water Works is the largest publicly traded water and wastewater utility in the United States, providing drinking water, wastewater, and other related services to approximately 14 million people across 14 states. The company operates regulated and market-based water services.
AXON: Axon Enterprise develops technology and weapon products for law enforcement and public safety, including TASER devices, body cameras, digital evidence management software (Axon Evidence), and records management systems. The company is the market leader in body-worn cameras for police departments.
AXP: American Express is a globally integrated payments company providing charge and credit card products, merchant acquisition and processing, network services, and travel-related services. AmEx differentiates through a premium brand, a closed-loop network, and a loyal customer base of affluent consumers and businesses.
AZO: AutoZone is the leading retailer and distributor of automotive replacement parts and accessories in the Americas. The company operates over 7,000 stores across the United States, Mexico, and Brazil, serving both do-it-yourself customers and professional mechanics.
BA: The Boeing Company is one of the world's two major commercial airplane manufacturers and a leading defense, space, and security contractor. Its commercial aviation division produces the 737, 767, 777, and 787 families of aircraft, while its defense unit builds military aircraft, satellites, and weapons systems.
BAC: Bank of America is one of the world's largest financial institutions, serving individual consumers, small businesses, large corporations, and governments with banking, investing, asset management, and risk management products. It is the second-largest bank in the United States by total assets.
BALL: Ball Corporation is a global supplier of sustainable aluminum packaging for beverages, personal care, and household products. The company is the world's largest manufacturer of recyclable aluminum beverage cans and cups, producing billions of containers annually across operations in North America, Europe, and South America.
BAX: Baxter International is a global medical products company focused on renal care, hospital products, and surgical care. The company provides products for kidney dialysis, IV solutions, infusion pumps, parenteral nutrition, and surgical hemostats and sealants used in hospitals worldwide.
BBWI: Bath & Body Works is a specialty retailer of home fragrance, body care, and soap products. The company operates approximately 1,800 company-owned stores in the United States and Canada and sells through its e-commerce platforms. Key product categories include candles, body lotions, shower gels, and hand soaps.
BBY: Best Buy is the largest specialty retailer of consumer electronics, computing, and appliance products in the United States. The company operates over 1,000 stores and a significant e-commerce business, offering products from major brands alongside Geek Squad installation and repair services.
BDX: Becton, Dickinson and Company is a global medical technology company that manufactures and sells medical devices, instrument systems, and reagents. Its products include syringes, needles, IV catheters, blood collection systems, diagnostic instruments, and medication management solutions used in healthcare settings worldwide.
BEN: Franklin Resources, operating as Franklin Templeton, is a global investment management organization offering mutual funds, ETFs, and institutional investment products across equity, fixed income, alternative, and multi-asset strategies. The firm manages over $1.4 trillion in assets.
BG: Bunge Global SA is a leading agribusiness and food company operating in the farm-to-consumer food supply chain. The company buys, stores, transports, processes, and sells agricultural commodities including oilseeds, grains, and sugar, producing vegetable oils, protein meals, and milled grain products for food, animal feed, and biofuel markets.
BIIB: Biogen is a biotechnology company specializing in therapies for neurological and neurodegenerative diseases. The company's portfolio includes treatments for multiple sclerosis (Tecfidera, Tysabri), spinal muscular atrophy (Spinraza), and Alzheimer's disease (Leqembi, co-developed with Eisai).
BIO: Bio-Rad Laboratories develops and manufactures a broad range of products for life science research and clinical diagnostics. Its instruments, reagents, and software are used in genomics, proteomics, cell biology, and food safety testing, as well as blood typing, diabetes monitoring, and quality control in clinical labs.
BK: The Bank of New York Mellon is the world's largest custodian bank, providing investment servicing, investment management, and wealth management. BNY Mellon holds over $45 trillion in assets under custody and administers critical financial market infrastructure for institutional investors globally.
BKNG: Booking Holdings is the world's largest online travel company, operating platforms including Booking.com, Priceline, Kayak, Agoda, and OpenTable. The company connects travelers with the world's largest selection of accommodations, flights, rental cars, and restaurant reservations.
BKR: Baker Hughes is an energy technology company providing equipment and services for oil and gas exploration, production, and processing. The company operates across oilfield services, industrial energy technology, and digital solutions, serving customers across the upstream, midstream, and downstream energy sectors.
BLK: BlackRock is the world's largest asset manager with over $10 trillion in assets under management. The company provides investment management, risk management, and advisory services through its iShares ETF platform, actively managed funds, and the Aladdin technology platform used by institutional investors worldwide.
BMY: Bristol-Myers Squibb is a global biopharmaceutical company developing and delivering innovative medicines for serious diseases including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and immunological disorders. Key products include Opdivo and Yervoy in immuno-oncology, Eliquis for blood clots, and Revlimid for multiple myeloma.
BR: Broadridge Financial Solutions is a global fintech leader providing investor communications, technology-driven solutions, and data and analytics to banks, broker-dealers, asset managers, and corporate issuers. The company processes trillions of dollars in trades daily and distributes billions of investor communications annually.
BRK.B: Berkshire Hathaway is a multinational conglomerate holding company led by Warren Buffett, with diverse operations spanning insurance (GEICO, Gen Re), railroads (BNSF), energy (Berkshire Hathaway Energy), manufacturing, retail, and a massive public equity portfolio including significant stakes in Apple, Coca-Cola, and American Express.
BRO: Brown & Brown is one of the largest independent insurance brokerage firms in the United States, providing risk management solutions including property and casualty insurance, employee benefits, and personal insurance. The company operates through a network of offices across the U.S., U.K., and Bermuda.
BSX: Boston Scientific is a global medical device manufacturer developing innovative solutions for interventional cardiology, cardiac rhythm management, endoscopy, urology, and neuromodulation. The company's products include stents, pacemakers, defibrillators, and minimally invasive surgical tools used in hospitals worldwide.
BWA: BorgWarner is a global supplier of powertrain components and systems for combustion, hybrid, and electric vehicles. The company produces turbochargers, transmission components, electric motors, battery systems, and thermal management solutions for major automakers transitioning to electrification.
BX: Blackstone is the world's largest alternative asset manager with over $1 trillion in assets under management across real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, and hedge fund strategies. The company serves institutional and individual investors and is a major owner of commercial real estate globally.
BXP: BXP (formerly Boston Properties) is the largest publicly traded developer, owner, and manager of Class A office properties in the United States. The company's portfolio includes premier workplaces in Boston, Los Angeles, New York, San Francisco, Seattle, and Washington, D.C.
C: Citigroup is a global diversified financial services company providing consumer banking, corporate and investment banking, securities brokerage, and wealth management. Operating in approximately 160 countries, Citi has one of the most extensive global banking networks and is a major player in cross-border transactions.
CAH: Cardinal Health is a global healthcare services and products company providing pharmaceutical distribution, medical products, and solutions to hospitals, pharmacies, and healthcare providers. It is one of the three largest pharmaceutical distributors in the United States by revenue.
CARR: Carrier Global Corporation is a leading global provider of heating, ventilating, air conditioning (HVAC), refrigeration, fire and security solutions. The company was spun off from United Technologies in 2020 and serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers worldwide.
CAT: Caterpillar is the world's largest manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives. The company sells machinery and equipment through a global dealer network spanning 190 countries.
CB: Chubb Limited is the world's largest publicly traded property and casualty insurance company, offering commercial and personal property and casualty insurance, personal accident and supplemental health insurance, reinsurance, and life insurance across 54 countries.
CBOE: Cboe Global Markets is a leading provider of market infrastructure and tradable products, operating exchanges for options, equities, futures, and foreign exchange across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific. The company created the VIX volatility index and operates the largest U.S. options exchange.
CBRE: CBRE Group is the world's largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, offering property sales and leasing, facilities management, project management, mortgage financing, and real estate investment management to owners, investors, and occupiers globally.
CCI: Crown Castle is a REIT that owns, operates, and leases over 40,000 cell towers and approximately 85,000 route miles of fiber supporting small cells and fiber solutions across the United States. The company provides critical wireless infrastructure to all major U.S. carriers.
CCL: Carnival Corporation is the world's largest cruise line operator, with a portfolio of nine brands including Carnival Cruise Line, Princess Cruises, Holland America Line, and Costa Cruises. The company operates over 90 ships with approximately 270,000 lower berths, visiting over 700 ports worldwide.
CDNS: Cadence Design Systems provides electronic design automation (EDA) software, hardware, and services used to design integrated circuits, systems on chips, and electronic systems. The company's tools are essential for semiconductor companies designing the next generation of chips.
CDW: CDW Corporation is a leading provider of technology solutions for business, government, education, and healthcare. The company offers hardware, software, cloud computing, and IT services through a team of technology specialists and a broad vendor partner ecosystem.
CE: Celanese Corporation is a global chemical and specialty materials company producing engineered polymers, acetyl chain products, and other advanced materials. Its products are used in automotive, electronics, medical, consumer goods, and industrial applications worldwide.
CEG: Constellation Energy is the largest producer of carbon-free energy in the United States, generating electricity primarily from nuclear power plants. The company operates the largest fleet of nuclear reactors in the country and also produces power from solar, wind, and hydroelectric sources.
CF: CF Industries is one of the world's largest manufacturers and distributors of nitrogen-based fertilizer products, including ammonia, granular urea, and urea ammonium nitrate solutions. The company operates major nitrogen manufacturing complexes in North America and the United Kingdom.
CFG: Citizens Financial Group is a regional bank holding company providing retail and commercial banking products and services across the northeastern and midwestern United States. The company offers mortgages, student lending, credit cards, wealth management, and capital markets services.
CHD: Church & Dwight is a consumer products company manufacturing and marketing household, personal care, and specialty products. Its brands include Arm & Hammer, OxiClean, Trojan, First Response, Batiste, and Therabreath, sold primarily in the United States and international markets.
CHRW: C.H. Robinson Worldwide is one of the world's largest third-party logistics companies, providing freight transportation, logistics, and supply chain management services. The company connects shippers with carriers through its proprietary technology platform, handling truckload, less-than-truckload, ocean, and air shipments.
CHTR: Charter Communications is the second-largest cable operator in the United States, serving over 30 million customers under the Spectrum brand. The company provides broadband internet, cable television, mobile phone service, and voice communications across 41 states.
CI: The Cigna Group is a global health services company providing medical, dental, behavioral health, pharmacy, and vision care benefits. Through its Evernorth Health Services division, the company operates one of the largest pharmacy benefit managers (Express Scripts) in the United States.
CIEN: Ciena Corporation is a networking systems, services, and software company providing hardware and software solutions for optical and packet networking. The company's products enable telecommunications service providers, cable operators, and enterprises to build and operate high-performance networks.
CINF: Cincinnati Financial is a property casualty insurance holding company that markets business, home, and auto insurance through independent agents in 46 states. The company is known for its strong relationships with independent insurance agents and its significant investment portfolio.
CL: Colgate-Palmolive is a global consumer products company focused on oral care, personal care, home care, and pet nutrition. Its brands include Colgate toothpaste (the world's number one), Palmolive dish soap, Ajax cleaners, and Hill's Science Diet pet food.
CLX: The Clorox Company manufactures and markets consumer and professional products including bleach, cleaning supplies, and food brands. Key brands include Clorox, Pine-Sol, Glad, Burt's Bees, Hidden Valley, and Kingsford charcoal, sold primarily in the United States.
CMCSA: Comcast Corporation is a global media and technology conglomerate. It is the largest cable television and internet provider in the United States through Xfinity, owns NBCUniversal (including NBC, Universal Pictures, and theme parks), and operates Sky, a leading European media company.
CME: CME Group operates the world's largest financial derivatives exchange, offering trading in futures and options across interest rates, equity indexes, foreign exchange, energy, agricultural commodities, and metals. The company's exchanges include CME, CBOT, NYMEX, and COMEX.
CMG: Chipotle Mexican Grill operates a chain of fast-casual restaurants specializing in burritos, bowls, tacos, and salads made with fresh ingredients. The company operates over 3,500 restaurants across the United States, Canada, and Europe, emphasizing food with integrity and responsibly sourced ingredients.
CMI: Cummins Inc. designs, manufactures, and distributes diesel and natural gas engines, electric and hybrid powertrains, filtration products, and power generation systems. The company serves the commercial vehicle, industrial, mining, and power generation markets globally and is investing heavily in hydrogen and battery-electric technologies.
CMS: CMS Energy is a Michigan-based energy company providing electric and natural gas utility services to approximately 6.8 million residents through its subsidiary Consumers Energy. The company is investing in renewable energy and infrastructure modernization to support Michigan's clean energy transition.
CNC: Centene Corporation is a managed care company providing healthcare programs and services to government-sponsored and commercially insured programs. The company is the largest Medicaid managed care organization in the United States and also serves Medicare Advantage, health insurance marketplace, and military populations.
CNP: CenterPoint Energy is a utility company delivering electric transmission and distribution, natural gas distribution, and energy-related services to approximately 7 million customers in Indiana, Louisiana, Minnesota, Mississippi, Ohio, Oklahoma, and Texas.
COF: Capital One Financial is a diversified financial services company specializing in credit cards, consumer banking, and commercial banking. It is one of the largest credit card issuers in the United States and has built a reputation as a technology-driven bank with significant digital banking operations.
COIN: Coinbase Global operates the largest cryptocurrency exchange in the United States, providing a platform for buying, selling, transferring, and storing digital currencies including Bitcoin and Ethereum. The company also offers institutional trading, staking services, and a self-custody wallet.
COP: ConocoPhillips is the world's largest independent exploration and production company based on production and reserves. The company explores for, produces, transports, and markets crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids across North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia.
COR: Cencora (formerly AmerisourceBergen) is a pharmaceutical sourcing and distribution services company, operating as one of the three largest drug wholesalers in the United States. The company distributes branded and generic pharmaceuticals, over-the-counter products, and home healthcare supplies to pharmacies, hospitals, and healthcare providers.
COST: Costco Wholesale operates a chain of membership-only warehouse clubs offering a wide selection of merchandise at low prices. The company is the third-largest retailer in the world, operating over 870 warehouses globally and generating high member loyalty through its value proposition and Kirkland Signature private label brand.
CPAY: Corpay (formerly FLEETCOR Technologies) is a global business payments company specializing in vehicle fleet payments, corporate payments, and cross-border payment solutions. The company processes billions of dollars in payments annually for businesses across over 100 countries.
CPB: Campbell Soup Company (now Campbell's) manufactures and markets branded food and beverage products, including Campbell's soups, Goldfish crackers, Pepperidge Farm baked goods, Prego pasta sauces, V8 juices, and Snyder's of Hanover pretzels, sold primarily in the United States.
CPRT: Copart operates the world's largest online vehicle auction platform, specializing in the remarketing and resale of salvage, used, and wholesale vehicles. The company provides services to insurance companies, dealers, fleet operators, and individual sellers through its online auction technology.
CPT: Camden Property Trust is a REIT that owns, develops, and manages multifamily apartment communities across the United States. The company's portfolio includes approximately 58,000 apartment homes in high-growth Sun Belt markets including Houston, Dallas, Tampa, and Nashville.
CRH: CRH plc is the largest building materials company in the world, providing essential materials for the construction and maintenance of infrastructure, housing, and commercial projects. The company produces aggregates, cement, readymixed concrete, and asphalt across North America and Europe.
CRM: Salesforce is the world's leading customer relationship management (CRM) platform, providing cloud-based software for sales, service, marketing, commerce, and analytics. The company has expanded into AI through Einstein and Data Cloud, and its platform serves businesses of all sizes across virtually every industry.
CRWD: CrowdStrike is a cybersecurity company providing cloud-native endpoint protection, threat intelligence, and incident response services through its Falcon platform. The company uses AI and machine learning to detect and prevent security breaches, serving enterprises, governments, and small businesses worldwide.
CSCO: Cisco Systems is the world's largest networking equipment company, providing hardware, software, and services for enterprise networking, cybersecurity, collaboration, and data center infrastructure. Its products include routers, switches, firewalls, video conferencing systems, and cloud-based networking platforms.
CSGP: CoStar Group is the leading provider of commercial real estate information, analytics, and online marketplaces. The company operates CoStar, the largest database of commercial real estate information, and Apartments.com, the leading online apartment marketplace in the United States.
CSX: CSX Corporation is one of the largest Class I railroads in North America, operating approximately 20,000 miles of track across 23 states and connecting major metropolitan markets in the eastern United States. The railroad transports intermodal containers, coal, chemicals, agricultural products, and manufactured goods.
CTAS: Cintas Corporation provides corporate identity uniforms and workplace supplies, including uniform rental and facility services, first aid and safety products, fire protection services, and document management. The company serves over one million businesses across North America.
CTRA: Coterra Energy is an independent exploration and production company with operations in the Permian Basin, Marcellus Shale, and Anadarko Basin. The company produces crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids, with a balanced portfolio of oil and gas assets across premier U.S. basins.
CTSH: Cognizant Technology Solutions is a global IT services and consulting company providing digital transformation, technology modernization, and business process services. The company serves clients in financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, and technology, with significant delivery operations in India.
CTVA: Corteva Agriscience is a global pure-play agriculture company providing seed and crop protection solutions to farmers worldwide. The company was spun off from DowDuPont in 2019 and offers seeds for corn, soybeans, and other crops, along with herbicides, insecticides, and fungicides.
CVNA: Carvana is an e-commerce platform for buying, selling, and financing used cars. The company offers a fully online car-buying experience with home delivery, operates car vending machines in select cities, and provides vehicle trade-ins, financing, and insurance products through its digital platform.
CVS: CVS Health is a diversified health services company integrating retail pharmacy, pharmacy benefits management (CVS Caremark), health insurance (Aetna), and primary care clinics (Oak Street Health, MinuteClinic). The company operates nearly 10,000 retail pharmacy locations across the United States.
CVX: Chevron Corporation is one of the world's largest integrated energy companies, engaged in the exploration, production, refining, and marketing of oil and natural gas. The company operates across the full energy value chain and is investing in lower-carbon technologies including hydrogen, carbon capture, and renewable fuels.
CZR: Caesars Entertainment is one of the largest gaming and hospitality companies in the world, operating over 50 properties across the United States under brands including Caesars Palace, Harrah's, and Horseshoe. The company also operates Caesars Sportsbook, a major sports betting platform.
D: Dominion Energy is a large regulated electric and natural gas utility serving approximately 7 million customers across 15 states. The company generates, transmits, and distributes electricity and distributes natural gas, with operations concentrated in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina.
DAL: Delta Air Lines is one of the world's largest airlines, serving over 200 million passengers annually across more than 275 destinations in 50+ countries. The company operates major hubs in Atlanta, Minneapolis, Detroit, Salt Lake City, Seattle, New York, Los Angeles, and Amsterdam.
DASH: DoorDash is the largest food delivery platform in the United States, connecting consumers with restaurants, grocery stores, and convenience stores. The company also operates in international markets and has expanded into grocery, alcohol, and convenience delivery through DashPass subscription service.
DD: DuPont de Nemours is a specialty materials and chemicals company providing engineered polymers, electronic materials, water purification products, and industrial solutions. The company serves the electronics, transportation, construction, healthcare, and water treatment industries worldwide.
DDOG: Datadog is a cloud-based monitoring and analytics platform that provides observability for cloud-scale applications. The company's products cover infrastructure monitoring, application performance monitoring, log management, and security, serving thousands of enterprise customers in their digital transformation efforts.
DE: Deere & Company (John Deere) is the world's largest manufacturer of agricultural machinery, including tractors, combines, planters, and harvesters. The company also produces construction and forestry equipment, and has become a leader in precision agriculture technology integrating GPS, AI, and autonomous systems.
DECK: Deckers Outdoor Corporation designs and markets footwear and apparel brands including UGG, HOKA, Teva, and Sanuk. HOKA has emerged as a high-growth brand in the performance running shoe market, while UGG remains a dominant brand in premium casual and comfort footwear.
DELL: Dell Technologies is a global technology company providing PCs, servers, storage, networking, and IT services to enterprises, governments, and consumers. The company is a leader in commercial PCs and enterprise infrastructure, and has become a major supplier of AI-optimized servers through its PowerEdge platform.
DFS: Discover Financial Services operates the Discover card payment network and offers credit cards, personal loans, student loans, and banking products. The company is the fourth-largest credit card issuer in the United States and operates its own payment network competing with Visa, Mastercard, and American Express.
DG: Dollar General is a discount retailer operating over 19,000 stores across the United States, primarily serving rural and suburban communities. The company offers consumable products, seasonal items, home products, and apparel at everyday low prices, with most items priced at $10 or less.
DGX: Quest Diagnostics is the largest provider of clinical laboratory services in the United States, performing medical tests that aid in the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of disease. The company processes approximately 500 million tests annually from its nationwide network of laboratories and patient service centers.
DHI: D.R. Horton is the largest homebuilder in the United States by volume, constructing and selling single-family homes across 33 states. The company offers homes across multiple price points through its D.R. Horton, Emerald Homes, Express Homes, and Freedom Homes brands.
DHR: Danaher Corporation is a global science and technology company providing instruments, equipment, software, and services across life sciences, diagnostics, water quality, and product identification. Its portfolio includes brands like Beckman Coulter, Cepheid, Pall, and Hach.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company is a global entertainment conglomerate operating theme parks and resorts (Walt Disney World, Disneyland), media networks (ABC, ESPN), film studios (Walt Disney, Pixar, Marvel, Lucasfilm), and streaming services (Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+). Disney is one of the most recognizable brands in the world.
DLR: Digital Realty Trust is a REIT that owns, acquires, develops, and operates data center campuses worldwide. The company provides colocation, interconnection, and cloud-enabling solutions to enterprise and cloud customers, operating over 300 data centers across six continents.
DLTR: Dollar Tree is a discount retailer operating over 16,000 stores under the Dollar Tree and Family Dollar banners across North America. Dollar Tree stores offer merchandise at fixed low price points, while Family Dollar provides a broader assortment of everyday essentials in lower-income communities.
DOV: Dover Corporation is a diversified global manufacturer providing innovative equipment, components, and solutions for a variety of industrial applications. The company operates through five segments: Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, Imaging & Identification, Pumps & Process Solutions, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies.
DOW: Dow Inc. is a global materials science company producing plastics, industrial intermediates, coatings, and silicones. The company serves packaging, infrastructure, mobility, and consumer care markets with products derived from its integrated manufacturing operations across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.
DPZ: Domino's Pizza is one of the world's largest pizza delivery companies, operating through a network of over 20,000 company-owned and franchise stores across more than 90 markets. The company has invested heavily in delivery technology, digital ordering, and supply chain logistics.
DRI: Darden Restaurants is the largest full-service restaurant company in the United States, operating over 2,000 restaurants under brands including Olive Garden, LongHorn Steakhouse, Yard House, The Capital Grille, Seasons 52, Bahama Breeze, Eddie V's, and Ruth's Chris Steak House.
DTE: DTE Energy is a diversified energy company with regulated electric and natural gas utilities serving approximately 3.4 million customers in Michigan. The company also operates non-utility energy businesses including energy trading, industrial energy services, and renewable energy projects.
DUK: Duke Energy is one of the largest electric power holding companies in the United States, providing electricity to approximately 8.2 million customers across the Carolinas, Florida, Indiana, Ohio, and Kentucky. The company is investing in grid modernization and clean energy transition.
DVN: Devon Energy is an independent oil and natural gas exploration and production company focused on the U.S. onshore, with operations in the Delaware Basin, Anadarko Basin, Eagle Ford, and Williston Basin. The company pioneered the fixed-plus-variable dividend framework adopted by many E&P peers.
DXCM: DexCom designs and markets continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems for people with diabetes. The company's sensors provide real-time glucose readings without fingersticks, transmitting data to smartphones and smartwatches. DexCom is the market leader in CGM technology alongside Abbott's FreeStyle Libre.
EA: Electronic Arts is one of the world's leading video game publishers, known for franchises including FIFA/EA Sports FC, Madden NFL, The Sims, Battlefield, and Apex Legends. The company develops and distributes games across consoles, PC, and mobile platforms, with growing revenue from live services and digital content.
EBAY: eBay operates a global e-commerce marketplace connecting buyers and sellers of new and used goods. The platform facilitates transactions across categories including electronics, fashion, collectibles, and automotive parts, serving millions of active buyers and sellers in approximately 190 markets worldwide.
ECL: Ecolab is the global leader in water treatment, hygiene, and infection prevention solutions for the foodservice, healthcare, hospitality, and industrial markets. The company helps customers maintain clean and safe environments while optimizing water and energy use across over 170 countries.
ED: Consolidated Edison is a regulated utility providing electric, gas, and steam service to approximately 10 million people in the New York metropolitan area. Con Edison is one of the oldest utilities in the United States, operating critical energy infrastructure in New York City and Westchester County.
EFX: Equifax is a global data, analytics, and technology company providing credit information, workforce solutions, and fraud prevention services. The company maintains one of the largest consumer and commercial credit databases in the world and serves businesses, governments, and consumers across 24 countries.
EIX: Edison International is the parent company of Southern California Edison, one of the largest electric utilities in the United States. The company provides electricity to approximately 15 million people across a 50,000 square-mile service territory in central, coastal, and southern California.
EL: The Estee Lauder Companies is one of the world's leading manufacturers and marketers of prestige skin care, makeup, fragrance, and hair care products. Its portfolio includes brands such as Estee Lauder, Clinique, MAC, La Mer, Bobbi Brown, and Jo Malone London.
ELV: Elevance Health (formerly Anthem) is one of the largest health insurance companies in the United States, affiliated with the Blue Cross Blue Shield Association. The company serves over 45 million members through its health plans and provides pharmacy benefit management, behavioral health, and analytics services.
EME: EMCOR Group is one of the largest electrical and mechanical construction and facilities services firms in the United States. The company provides construction, renovation, maintenance, and building services for commercial, industrial, utility, and institutional customers.
EMR: Emerson Electric is a global technology and engineering company providing automation solutions for industrial, commercial, and consumer markets. The company's products include process control systems, industrial valves, measurement instruments, and climate technologies used in manufacturing and infrastructure.
ENPH: Enphase Energy is a leading provider of microinverter-based solar and battery storage systems for residential and commercial applications. The company's microinverters convert DC power from individual solar panels to AC power, offering monitoring, communication, and control capabilities at the panel level.
EOG: EOG Resources is one of the largest independent oil and gas companies in the United States, focused on crude oil and natural gas exploration and production. The company operates primarily in prolific U.S. shale basins including the Permian Basin, Eagle Ford, and the Powder River Basin.
EQIX: Equinix is the world's largest data center REIT and colocation provider, operating over 260 data centers across 70+ metros on six continents. The company provides interconnection services that enable businesses to connect directly to partners, customers, and cloud providers.
EQR: Equity Residential is one of the largest publicly traded apartment REITs in the United States, focused on the acquisition, development, and management of high-quality apartment communities in urban and high-density suburban markets including Boston, New York, San Francisco, Seattle, and Denver.
EQT: EQT Corporation is the largest natural gas producer in the United States, focused on the Appalachian Basin. The company's operations span across Pennsylvania, West Virginia, and Ohio, where it produces Marcellus and Utica shale natural gas.
ES: Eversource Energy is a regulated utility serving approximately 4.4 million customers in Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire. The company provides electric distribution, natural gas distribution, and water services, and has invested in offshore wind energy projects in the northeastern United States.
ESS: Essex Property Trust is a REIT that develops, acquires, and manages apartment communities along the West Coast of the United States. The company's portfolio is concentrated in supply-constrained markets in Southern California, Northern California, and the Seattle metro area.
ETN: Eaton Corporation is a diversified power management company providing electrical, aerospace, vehicle, and eMobility products. The company helps customers manage power efficiently in data centers, utility infrastructure, commercial buildings, and vehicles, with growing exposure to electrification trends.
ETR: Entergy Corporation is an electric utility company generating and distributing electricity to approximately 3 million customers in Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas. The company operates a diverse generation fleet including nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy plants.
ETSY: Etsy operates a global online marketplace focused on handmade, vintage, and unique goods. The platform connects millions of creative entrepreneurs with buyers seeking distinctive items including jewelry, home decor, clothing, and craft supplies. Etsy also owns Depop and Reverb.
EVRG: Evergy is a regulated electric utility serving approximately 1.7 million customers in Kansas and Missouri. The company was formed from the merger of Great Plains Energy and Westar Energy and operates a generation fleet that is transitioning toward increasing renewable energy capacity.
EW: Edwards Lifesciences is a global leader in structural heart disease solutions and critical care monitoring. The company pioneered transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) technology and produces heart valves, hemodynamic monitoring systems, and surgical structural heart products used in cardiac procedures worldwide.
EXC: Exelon is the largest regulated utility company in the United States by customer count, serving approximately 10 million electricity and natural gas customers through its six utilities: ComEd (Illinois), PECO (Pennsylvania), BGE (Maryland), Pepco (D.C.), Delmarva Power, and Atlantic City Electric.
EXE: Expand Energy Corporation is a natural gas-focused exploration and production company formed from the merger of Chesapeake Energy and Southwestern Energy. The company is one of the largest natural gas producers in the United States with operations in the Marcellus, Haynesville, and Eagle Ford shale plays.
EXPD: Expeditors International of Washington is a global logistics company providing air and ocean freight forwarding, customs brokerage, warehousing, and distribution services. The company operates through a worldwide network of offices and partners, serving importers and exporters across all major trade lanes.
EXPE: Expedia Group is a global online travel company operating platforms including Expedia, Hotels.com, Vrbo, Orbitz, Travelocity, and Trivago. The company connects travelers with accommodations, flights, car rentals, cruises, and activities through its portfolio of consumer-facing and B2B travel brands.
EXR: Extra Space Storage is one of the largest self-storage REITs in the United States, owning or operating approximately 3,500 self-storage properties across 42 states and Washington, D.C. The company provides storage solutions to residential and commercial customers.
F: Ford Motor Company is one of the world's largest automakers, producing cars, trucks, SUVs, and electric vehicles under the Ford and Lincoln brands. The company's F-Series pickup has been America's best-selling truck for over four decades. Ford is investing heavily in electric vehicles and autonomous driving technology.
FANG: Diamondback Energy is an independent oil and natural gas company focused on the acquisition, development, and exploration of unconventional reserves in the Permian Basin in West Texas. The company is one of the largest pure-play Permian Basin operators by production volume.
FAST: Fastenal Company is a leading distributor of industrial and construction supplies, including fasteners, safety products, cutting tools, and facility maintenance supplies. The company operates through a network of over 3,200 branch and customer-site locations across North America.
FCX: Freeport-McMoRan is the world's largest publicly traded copper producer, operating major mining assets including the Grasberg mine in Indonesia and significant operations in the Americas. Copper is a critical metal for electrification, making the company a key beneficiary of the energy transition.
FDX: FedEx Corporation is a global transportation, e-commerce, and business services company providing express delivery, ground shipping, freight transportation, and logistics services. The company operates one of the world's largest air cargo fleets and delivers to over 220 countries and territories.
FE: FirstEnergy is a regulated electric utility company serving approximately 6 million customers in Ohio, Pennsylvania, New Jersey, West Virginia, Maryland, and New York. The company focuses on regulated transmission and distribution of electricity through its 10 utility subsidiaries.
FI: Fiserv is a global fintech and payments company providing core processing, digital banking, payment processing, and merchant acquiring solutions to financial institutions and businesses. The company processes billions of transactions annually through its Clover point-of-sale platform and card processing networks.
FICO: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) is the creator of the FICO Score, the most widely used credit score in the United States, used in over 90% of lending decisions. The company also provides analytics software and decision management solutions for fraud detection, customer engagement, and credit risk assessment.
FIS: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) is a global financial technology company providing banking, capital markets, and merchant payment solutions. The company processes trillions of dollars in transactions annually and serves financial institutions, businesses, and developers worldwide.
FISV: Fiserv is a global leader in payments and financial technology, providing account processing, digital banking, card processing, merchant acquiring, and billing solutions. The company serves banks, credit unions, and merchants, processing billions of transactions annually through its Clover and Carat platforms.
FITB: Fifth Third Bancorp is a diversified financial services company headquartered in Cincinnati, providing commercial banking, branch banking, consumer lending, and wealth and asset management across 11 states in the midwestern and southeastern United States.
FIX: Comfort Systems USA is a leading provider of commercial and industrial HVAC, plumbing, piping, and electrical installation and maintenance services. The company operates through a network of subsidiaries across the United States, serving healthcare, education, data center, and commercial construction markets.
FMC: FMC Corporation is a global agricultural sciences company producing crop protection chemicals including insecticides, herbicides, and fungicides. The company focuses on developing innovative active ingredients and formulation technologies to help farmers improve crop yields and manage pests.
FSLR: First Solar is the largest American solar panel manufacturer and the leading producer of thin-film photovoltaic modules. The company's cadmium telluride (CdTe) solar technology offers advantages in hot climates and has a lower carbon footprint compared to traditional crystalline silicon panels.
FTNT: Fortinet is a global cybersecurity company providing network security appliances, software, and services. Its FortiGate next-generation firewall is the most widely deployed network firewall in the world, and the company offers an integrated security platform covering firewalls, endpoint protection, and cloud security.
FTV: Fortive Corporation is a diversified industrial technology company providing instruments, software, and services for field solutions, product realization, and sensing technologies. Key brands include Fluke testing and measurement instruments, Tektronix oscilloscopes, and Qualitrol asset monitoring systems.
GD: General Dynamics is a global aerospace and defense company operating across four segments: aerospace (Gulfstream business jets), marine systems (nuclear submarines, surface combatants), combat systems (tanks, armored vehicles), and technologies (IT services, mission-critical systems). The company is a major contractor for the U.S. Department of Defense.
GE: GE Aerospace (formerly General Electric's aviation division) is one of the world's leading providers of jet engines, components, and integrated systems for commercial and military aircraft. The company's engines, including the LEAP and GE9X, power a significant portion of the global commercial aircraft fleet.
GEHC: GE HealthCare Technologies provides medical imaging, ultrasound, patient monitoring, diagnostics, and pharmaceutical manufacturing solutions. The company was spun off from General Electric in 2023 and serves hospitals, clinics, and pharmaceutical companies with products including MRI machines, CT scanners, and ultrasound systems.
GEV: GE Vernova is a global energy company providing power generation, wind energy, electrification, and energy technology solutions. Spun off from General Electric in 2024, the company manufactures gas turbines, onshore and offshore wind turbines, grid solutions, and power conversion equipment.
GILD: Gilead Sciences is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing antiviral therapies. The company revolutionized hepatitis C treatment with its curative therapies (Sovaldi, Harvoni) and is a leader in HIV treatment and prevention with its Biktarvy and Descovy products.
GIS: General Mills is a global food company producing and marketing branded consumer foods including Cheerios, Nature Valley, Pillsbury, Haagen-Dazs, Betty Crocker, and Blue Buffalo pet food. The company sells products in over 100 countries across cereal, snacks, baking, meals, yogurt, and pet food categories.
GLW: Corning Incorporated is a materials science company specializing in glass, ceramics, and optical physics. The company produces Gorilla Glass for smartphones and tablets, optical fiber for telecommunications, display glass for TVs and monitors, and ceramic substrates for automotive emission control systems.
GM: General Motors is one of the world's largest automakers, manufacturing vehicles under the Chevrolet, GMC, Cadillac, and Buick brands. The company is investing heavily in electric vehicles through its Ultium platform and the GMC Hummer EV, Chevrolet Equinox EV, and Cadillac Lyriq models.
GNRC: Generac Holdings is a leading designer and manufacturer of a wide range of energy technology solutions including backup power generators, energy storage systems, and grid services. The company's products serve residential, commercial, and industrial markets, with growing exposure to clean energy and grid resilience.
GOOG: Alphabet Inc. (Class C shares) is the parent company of Google, the world's dominant search engine, and YouTube, the largest video-sharing platform. Alphabet also operates Google Cloud, the Android mobile operating system, Waymo autonomous vehicles, and various other technology ventures.
GOOGL: Alphabet Inc. (Class A shares) is the parent company of Google, the world's dominant search engine commanding over 90% global market share. The company generates most of its revenue from digital advertising across Google Search, YouTube, and its ad network. Alphabet also operates Google Cloud, Android, and Waymo.
GPN: Global Payments is a worldwide payments technology company providing payment processing, software, and commerce solutions to merchants, issuers, and consumers. The company merged with Total System Services (TSYS) and processes billions of transactions annually across over 100 countries.
GRMN: Garmin designs and manufactures GPS navigation and wearable technology devices for the fitness, outdoor, marine, aviation, and automotive markets. The company is a leader in GPS-enabled smartwatches, cycling computers, marine electronics, and aviation avionics, known for durability and battery life.
GS: The Goldman Sachs Group is a leading global financial institution providing investment banking, securities, asset management, and consumer banking services. The company is the world's preeminent M&A advisory firm and a major player in trading, underwriting, and institutional asset management.
GWW: W.W. Grainger is a leading broad-line distributor of maintenance, repair, and operating (MRO) products and services. The company provides over 2 million products including safety equipment, tools, motors, lighting, and janitorial supplies to businesses and institutions across North America and Japan.
HAL: Halliburton is one of the world's largest oilfield services companies, providing drilling, evaluation, completion, production, and intervention services to oil and gas operators. The company operates in over 70 countries and offers technologies for unconventional resource development and deepwater operations.
HBAN: Huntington Bancshares is a regional bank holding company headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, providing consumer, small business, commercial, treasury management, and wealth management services across 11 midwestern and Great Lakes states.
HCA: HCA Healthcare is the largest investor-owned hospital operator in the United States, operating approximately 180 hospitals and over 2,300 ambulatory surgery centers, freestanding emergency rooms, and urgent care facilities across 20 states and the United Kingdom.
HD: The Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement retailer, operating over 2,300 stores across North America. The company sells building materials, home improvement products, lawn and garden supplies, and decor, serving both do-it-yourself customers and professional contractors.
HES: Hess Corporation is an independent energy company engaged in the exploration and production of crude oil and natural gas. The company's key asset is its significant position in the Stabroek block offshore Guyana, one of the world's most important oil discoveries of the past decade.
HIG: The Hartford Financial Services Group is an insurance and financial services company offering property and casualty insurance, group benefits, and mutual funds. The company serves commercial, small business, and personal customers and is a leading provider of workers' compensation insurance.
HLT: Hilton Worldwide Holdings is one of the largest hospitality companies in the world, operating a portfolio of 22 brands including Hilton, Conrad, Waldorf Astoria, DoubleTree, Hampton, and Embassy Suites. The company manages and franchises over 7,500 properties across 126 countries.
HOLX: Hologic is a medical technology company primarily focused on women's health diagnostics and surgical products. The company produces mammography systems, cervical cancer screening tests, molecular diagnostics (including COVID-19 tests), and minimally invasive surgical devices.
HON: Honeywell International is a diversified technology and manufacturing company operating across four segments: aerospace technologies, industrial automation, building automation, and energy and sustainability solutions. The company produces aircraft engines, control systems, safety products, and advanced materials.
HOOD: Robinhood Markets operates a financial services platform that democratizes investing by offering commission-free trading of stocks, ETFs, options, and cryptocurrency. The company popularized zero-commission trading and mobile-first investing, primarily serving younger and first-time investors in the United States.
HPE: Hewlett Packard Enterprise provides technology solutions including servers, storage, networking, edge computing, and hybrid cloud services to enterprises and governments. The company focuses on helping organizations accelerate digital transformation through its GreenLake cloud platform and AI-optimized infrastructure.
HPQ: HP Inc. is one of the world's largest manufacturers of personal computers and printers, producing laptops, desktops, workstations, and a range of printing solutions for consumers and businesses. The company also provides 3D printing and digital manufacturing solutions.
HSIC: Henry Schein is the world's largest distributor of healthcare products and services to office-based dental and medical practitioners. The company provides supplies, equipment, technology, and business solutions to dental, medical, and animal health professionals globally.
HST: Host Hotels & Resorts is the largest lodging REIT in the United States, owning a portfolio of luxury and upper-upscale hotels operated primarily under the Marriott, Ritz-Carlton, Westin, and Hyatt brands. The company's properties are located in major urban and resort destinations.
HSY: The Hershey Company is one of the largest chocolate and confectionery manufacturers in the world. Its brands include Hershey's, Reese's, Kit Kat (U.S.), Jolly Rancher, Twizzlers, and SkinnyPop. The company also has a growing snacking portfolio and is expanding into international markets.
HUBB: Hubbell Incorporated is a manufacturer of electrical and utility products for commercial, industrial, residential, and utility applications. The company produces wiring devices, lighting fixtures, power distribution equipment, and utility transmission and distribution components used in buildings and electrical infrastructure.
HUM: Humana is a leading health insurance company focused on Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, commercial health plans, and specialty benefits. The company also operates CenterWell, an integrated health services platform providing primary care, pharmacy, and home health services.
HWM: Howmet Aerospace is a leading global provider of advanced engineered solutions for the aerospace and transportation industries. The company manufactures jet engine components, fastening systems, engineered structures, and forged wheels using advanced metallurgy and manufacturing processes for commercial and military applications.
IBKR: Interactive Brokers Group is a global electronic brokerage firm providing trading access to stocks, options, futures, currencies, bonds, funds, and cryptocurrencies across 150+ markets in 34 countries. The company is known for its low-cost trading, sophisticated trading tools, and serving professional and institutional traders.
IBM: International Business Machines provides hybrid cloud computing, AI (watsonx), consulting, and IT infrastructure solutions to enterprise clients worldwide. The company has pivoted from its legacy hardware business to focus on Red Hat cloud technologies, enterprise AI, and strategic consulting services.
ICE: Intercontinental Exchange operates a network of regulated exchanges, clearing houses, and data services for financial and commodity markets. The company owns the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), the world's largest stock exchange by market capitalization, and ICE Futures exchanges globally.
IDXX: IDEXX Laboratories is the global leader in veterinary diagnostics, providing instruments, consumables, and software for companion animal and livestock health testing. The company also provides water and dairy quality testing solutions. IDEXX's products are used by veterinary practices, reference laboratories, and livestock producers.
IFF: International Flavors & Fragrances is a global leader in food and beverage flavors, scent products, nutrition, and biosciences. The company creates flavors, fragrances, and ingredients used in consumer products ranging from foods and beverages to personal care, household products, and pharmaceuticals.
ILMN: Illumina is the world's leading maker of DNA sequencing instruments and consumables, enabling genomic research and clinical applications. The company's sequencing technology has driven down the cost of sequencing a human genome and is used for genetic testing, oncology, reproductive health, and drug development.
INCY: Incyte Corporation is a biopharmaceutical company focused on the discovery, development, and commercialization of novel therapeutics for oncology, inflammation, and autoimmune diseases. Its key product Jakafi (ruxolitinib) is the first FDA-approved treatment for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera.
INTC: Intel Corporation is one of the world's largest semiconductor companies, designing and manufacturing processors (Core, Xeon) for personal computers, data centers, and embedded systems. The company also operates a foundry business to manufacture chips for external customers and is investing heavily to rebuild its manufacturing competitiveness.
INTU: Intuit is a financial technology company providing tax preparation (TurboTax), small business accounting (QuickBooks), personal finance (Credit Karma), and email marketing (Mailchimp) solutions. The company serves approximately 100 million consumers, small businesses, and self-employed workers worldwide.
INVH: Invitation Homes is the largest single-family home rental company in the United States, owning and operating approximately 80,000 homes across 16 markets. The company provides professionally managed rental homes in desirable neighborhoods, primarily in the Sun Belt and western United States.
IP: International Paper is one of the world's largest producers of renewable fiber-based packaging and paper products. The company manufactures corrugated packaging, containerboard, and cellulose fibers used in diapers, tissue, and other absorbent products, serving customers in approximately 150 countries.
IQV: IQVIA Holdings provides advanced analytics, technology solutions, and contract research services to the life sciences industry. The company combines clinical trial management, real-world evidence, and commercial analytics to help pharmaceutical and biotech companies develop and commercialize new therapies.
IR: Ingersoll Rand is a global provider of mission-critical flow creation, industrial, and climate technologies. The company manufactures compressors, blowers, pumps, and fluid management systems for industrial, energy, medical, and environmental applications worldwide.
IRM: Iron Mountain is a REIT specializing in records management, data center, and information management services. The company stores and protects billions of records and data assets for over 225,000 customers worldwide, and is growing its data center business to serve cloud and enterprise computing needs.
ISRG: Intuitive Surgical is the global leader in robotic-assisted minimally invasive surgery, manufacturing the da Vinci and Ion surgical systems. The da Vinci system is used in over 10 million procedures across urology, gynecology, thoracic, cardiac, and general surgery worldwide.
IT: Gartner is the world's leading research and advisory company for information technology and business leaders. The company provides research, consulting, conferences, and peer networking to help executives make better decisions on technology strategy, operations, and digital transformation.
ITW: Illinois Tool Works is a diversified manufacturer of industrial products and equipment spanning seven segments including automotive, food equipment, test and measurement, welding, polymers, construction products, and specialty products. The company operates through a decentralized business model across 51 countries.
IVZ: Invesco is a global independent investment management firm offering a range of mutual funds, ETFs (including the QQQ Trust tracking the Nasdaq-100), and institutional investment strategies. The company manages over $1.6 trillion in assets across equity, fixed income, alternative, and multi-asset products.
JBHT: J.B. Hunt Transport Services is one of the largest transportation and logistics companies in North America, providing intermodal, truck brokerage, dedicated contract carriage, and final-mile delivery services. The company is the largest intermodal provider in North America through its partnership with railroads.
JBL: Jabil is a global manufacturing services company providing electronics design, production, and product management services to major technology brands. The company operates over 100 manufacturing facilities across 30 countries, serving the healthcare, automotive, consumer electronics, and cloud computing industries.
JCI: Johnson Controls International is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings, providing building automation, HVAC, fire and security solutions. The company's OpenBlue digital platform connects building systems to optimize energy usage, enhance occupant experience, and improve operational efficiency.
JNJ: Johnson & Johnson is a global healthcare company operating across three segments: innovative medicine (pharmaceuticals), MedTech (medical devices), and consumer health (recently spun off as Kenvue). The company's pharmaceutical portfolio includes oncology, immunology, and neuroscience therapies.
JPM: JPMorgan Chase is the largest bank in the United States and one of the largest financial institutions in the world by total assets. The company provides investment banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing, asset management, and private banking services to millions of customers globally.
K: Kellanova (formerly Kellogg) is a global snacking, cereal, and noodles company with brands including Pringles, Cheez-It, Pop-Tarts, Eggo, and MorningStar Farms. The company separated from WK Kellogg Co (North American cereal business) in 2023 to focus on its higher-growth snacking portfolio.
KDP: Keurig Dr Pepper is a major beverage company formed from the merger of Keurig Green Mountain and Dr Pepper Snapple. The company produces and distributes hot and cold beverages including Dr Pepper, 7UP, Snapple, and Green Mountain Coffee, and manufactures the Keurig single-serve brewing system.
KEY: KeyCorp is a bank holding company operating primarily through KeyBank National Association, providing commercial and consumer banking, investment management, and investment banking services across 15 states, with a focus on the Midwest and Northeast United States.
KEYS: Keysight Technologies provides electronic design and test solutions to the communications, industrial, aerospace, defense, automotive, semiconductor, and general electronics markets. The company's instruments include oscilloscopes, signal analyzers, network analyzers, and 5G test solutions.
KHC: The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, co-headquartered in Chicago and Pittsburgh. Its iconic brands include Heinz ketchup, Kraft mac and cheese, Oscar Mayer, Philadelphia cream cheese, Velveeta, Planters, and Jell-O.
KIM: Kimco Realty is a REIT focused on owning, operating, and developing open-air, grocery-anchored shopping centers and mixed-use assets primarily in the top U.S. metropolitan markets. The company's portfolio includes over 500 properties totaling approximately 90 million square feet.
KKR: KKR & Co. is a leading global investment firm managing approximately $600 billion in assets across private equity, credit, real estate, infrastructure, and energy strategies. The company also operates insurance businesses through Global Atlantic and provides capital markets solutions.
KLAC: KLA Corporation is a leading supplier of process control and yield management equipment for the semiconductor industry. The company's inspection and metrology tools detect defects and measure features during chip manufacturing, making its products essential for advanced semiconductor production.
KMB: Kimberly-Clark is a global consumer products company producing tissue, personal care, and professional products. Its brands include Huggies diapers, Kleenex tissues, Scott paper products, Cottonelle, and Depend. The company sells products in over 175 countries.
KMI: Kinder Morgan is one of the largest energy infrastructure companies in North America, operating approximately 83,000 miles of natural gas and liquids pipelines and 140 terminals. The company transports approximately 40% of the natural gas consumed in the United States.
KO: The Coca-Cola Company is the world's largest beverage company, producing and distributing over 200 brands including Coca-Cola, Diet Coke, Sprite, Fanta, Minute Maid, Dasani, and Costa Coffee. The company operates a franchise bottling system reaching consumers in virtually every country worldwide.
KR: The Kroger Co. is one of the largest grocery retailers in the United States, operating approximately 2,700 supermarkets and multi-department stores under brands including Kroger, Ralphs, Fred Meyer, Harris Teeter, and King Soopers. The company also operates fuel centers, pharmacies, and manufacturing facilities.
KVUE: Kenvue is a consumer health company spun off from Johnson & Johnson in 2023. The company owns iconic brands including Tylenol, Band-Aid, Listerine, Neutrogena, Aveeno, and Zyrtec, serving consumers worldwide with over-the-counter health, skin care, and personal care products.
