KWH: Kenvue is a consumer health company spun off from Johnson & Johnson in 2023. The company owns iconic brands including Tylenol, Band-Aid, Listerine, Neutrogena, Aveeno, and Zyrtec, serving consumers worldwide with over-the-counter health, skin care, and personal care products.
L: Loews Corporation is a diversified holding company with subsidiaries in energy (CNA Financial insurance, Boardwalk Pipelines natural gas transportation), hospitality (Loews Hotels), and packaging (Altium Packaging). The company is controlled by the Tisch family.
LDOS: Leidos Holdings is a technology, engineering, and science company providing services to the U.S. defense, intelligence, civil agencies, and health markets. The company's solutions include IT modernization, cybersecurity, data analytics, and systems engineering for government and commercial customers.
LEN: Lennar Corporation is one of the largest homebuilders in the United States, constructing and selling single-family attached and detached homes. The company operates in major markets across the country and has vertically integrated operations including mortgage financing, title insurance, and home technology.
LH: Labcorp Holdings is a leading global life sciences company providing laboratory testing and drug development services. The company operates one of the world's largest clinical laboratory networks and provides central laboratory, early development, and clinical trial management services to pharmaceutical companies.
LHX: L3Harris Technologies is a global aerospace and defense technology company providing advanced sensors, communication systems, electronic warfare, and space systems. The company serves military, government, and commercial customers with solutions spanning air, land, sea, space, and cyber domains.
LII: Lennox International is a global provider of climate control solutions, manufacturing heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration products. The company serves the residential comfort, commercial building, and commercial refrigeration markets through its Lennox, Armstrong Air, and Heatcraft brands.
LIN: Linde plc is the world's largest industrial gas company, supplying atmospheric gases (oxygen, nitrogen, argon), process gases (hydrogen, carbon dioxide), and engineering services. The company serves healthcare, food and beverage, electronics, energy, and manufacturing industries across approximately 100 countries.
LLY: Eli Lilly and Company is a global pharmaceutical company known for its leadership in diabetes care (Trulicity, Mounjaro), oncology, immunology, and neuroscience. The company's GLP-1 receptor agonist Mounjaro/Zepbound has become one of the fastest-growing drug franchises in history, treating both diabetes and obesity.
LMT: Lockheed Martin is the world's largest defense contractor, producing the F-35 Lightning II stealth fighter, Sikorsky helicopters, missile defense systems (THAAD, PAC-3), and space systems. The company is a primary contractor for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied nations.
LNT: Alliant Energy is a regulated utility holding company providing electric and natural gas service to approximately one million customers in Iowa and Wisconsin. The company is actively investing in renewable energy, particularly solar and wind, as part of its clean energy transition.
LOW: Lowe's Companies is the second-largest home improvement retailer in the world, operating approximately 1,700 stores across the United States and Canada. The company sells products for construction, maintenance, repair, remodeling, and decorating, serving both DIY consumers and professional contractors.
LRCX: Lam Research designs and manufactures semiconductor processing equipment used in the fabrication of integrated circuits. The company specializes in etch, deposition, and clean technologies that are critical for producing advanced memory and logic chips.
LULU: Lululemon Athletica is a premium athletic apparel company known for its yoga pants, running gear, and lifestyle clothing. The company operates over 650 stores worldwide and has built a passionate customer community through its focus on innovation, quality fabrics, and in-store experiences.
LUV: Southwest Airlines is a major U.S. airline known for its low-fare, no-frills model and point-to-point route network. The company operates the largest fleet of Boeing 737 aircraft and serves over 100 destinations across the United States and near-international markets, with no baggage fees and open seating.
LVS: Las Vegas Sands is a global developer and operator of integrated resort properties featuring casino gaming, convention and exhibition space, luxury hotels, and retail. The company operates The Venetian Macao, Marina Bay Sands in Singapore, and other properties across Asia.
LW: Lamb Weston Holdings is one of the world's largest producers of frozen potato products, including french fries, potato wedges, and specialty items. The company supplies major restaurant chains, foodservice distributors, and retail grocery customers globally.
LYB: LyondellBasell is one of the world's largest plastics, chemicals, and refining companies. The company produces polyethylene, polypropylene, and other plastics used in packaging, automotive, construction, and electronics, as well as refined products including gasoline and diesel fuel.
LYV: Live Nation Entertainment is the world's leading live entertainment company, comprising Ticketmaster (the largest ticketing platform), Live Nation Concerts (the largest concert promoter), and venue management operations. The company promotes over 40,000 events annually and operates or has booking rights at over 300 venues worldwide.
MA: Mastercard is a global payments technology company operating the world's second-largest payment processing network. The company connects consumers, financial institutions, merchants, and governments across more than 210 countries, processing billions of transactions annually through its credit, debit, and prepaid card network.
MAA: Mid-America Apartment Communities is a REIT focused on owning, operating, and developing apartment communities in the Sun Belt region of the United States. The company's portfolio includes approximately 100,000 apartment homes across markets including Atlanta, Dallas, Tampa, and Charlotte.
MAR: Marriott International is the world's largest hotel company, operating and franchising over 8,700 properties across 139 countries under 31 brands including Marriott, Ritz-Carlton, St. Regis, W Hotels, Westin, Sheraton, and Courtyard. The company's Bonvoy loyalty program has over 190 million members.
MAS: Masco Corporation is a global manufacturer of home improvement and building products, including Delta and Hansgrohe faucets, Behr paint, KraftMaid and Merillat cabinetry, and Milgard windows and doors. The company's products are sold through home centers, hardware stores, and builders.
MCD: McDonald's Corporation is the world's largest restaurant chain by revenue, operating and franchising over 40,000 restaurants in more than 100 countries. The company serves approximately 69 million customers daily through its menu of burgers, chicken, breakfast items, and beverages.
MCHP: Microchip Technology is a leading provider of microcontrollers, mixed-signal, analog, and flash-IP integrated circuits for embedded control applications. The company's products are used in automotive, industrial, consumer, aerospace, communications, and computing applications.
MCK: McKesson Corporation is the largest pharmaceutical distributor in the United States and a healthcare technology company. The company distributes pharmaceuticals, medical supplies, and healthcare IT solutions to pharmacies, hospitals, and healthcare providers across North America.
MCO: Moody's Corporation is a global provider of credit ratings, research, and risk analysis through Moody's Investors Service, and data analytics and risk management solutions through Moody's Analytics. The company is one of the three major credit rating agencies globally alongside S&P and Fitch.
MDB: MongoDB provides a leading NoSQL database platform used by developers to build modern applications. The company's document-based database (MongoDB Atlas cloud service and self-managed MongoDB Enterprise) powers applications for thousands of organizations including financial services, retail, and healthcare companies.
MDLZ: Mondelez International is a global snacking company with a portfolio of iconic brands including Oreo, Cadbury, Ritz, Toblerone, LU biscuits, and Trident gum. The company operates in approximately 150 countries and is one of the world's largest chocolate, biscuit, and baked snacks producers.
MDT: Medtronic is one of the world's largest medical device companies, developing and manufacturing therapies for cardiovascular, neuroscience, diabetes, and surgical applications. Key products include cardiac pacemakers, insulin pumps, spinal implants, and surgical navigation systems used in hospitals and clinics globally.
MELI: MercadoLibre is the largest e-commerce and fintech platform in Latin America, operating online marketplaces, digital payments (Mercado Pago), logistics (Mercado Envios), and credit services across 18 countries. The company is often referred to as the Amazon of Latin America.
MET: MetLife is one of the world's largest life insurance companies, providing life, dental, disability, and accident insurance, as well as annuities and employee benefits programs. The company serves approximately 90 million customers across more than 40 countries.
META: Meta Platforms is the parent company of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger, connecting billions of people worldwide through social networking and messaging. The company generates the majority of its revenue from digital advertising and is investing heavily in the metaverse through its Reality Labs division.
MGM: MGM Resorts International is a global hospitality and entertainment company operating iconic casino resorts including the Bellagio, MGM Grand, Mandalay Bay, and Aria on the Las Vegas Strip. The company also operates properties across the United States, China (MGM Macau and MGM Cotai), and online through BetMGM.
MLM: Martin Marietta Materials is a leading supplier of aggregates (crusite limestone, granite, sand, gravel) and heavy building materials used in the construction of roads, sidewalks, buildings, and other infrastructure. The company operates quarries and distribution yards across the United States.
MMC: Marsh & McLennan Companies is the world's largest insurance broker and risk advisor, operating through Marsh (insurance brokerage), Guy Carpenter (reinsurance), Mercer (HR consulting), and Oliver Wyman (management consulting). The company helps organizations navigate complex risks and workforce challenges.
MMM: 3M Company is a diversified technology and manufacturing conglomerate producing thousands of products including Post-it Notes, Scotch tape, industrial abrasives, automotive parts, healthcare supplies, and personal protective equipment. The company operates across safety and industrial, transportation and electronics, healthcare, and consumer segments.
MNST: Monster Beverage Corporation is a leading energy drink company, producing and marketing Monster Energy, Reign, and other energy and sports drinks. The company competes primarily with Red Bull and distributes its products through The Coca-Cola Company's bottling network in many markets.
MO: Altria Group is the largest tobacco company in the United States, producing and selling cigarettes (Marlboro, the world's best-selling cigarette brand), smokeless tobacco (Copenhagen, Skoal), and oral nicotine pouches (on!). The company also holds a stake in Anheuser-Busch InBev.
MOH: Molina Healthcare is a managed care company providing health insurance services under Medicaid and Medicare programs, as well as through the Health Insurance Marketplace. The company serves approximately 5.6 million members across 20 states, primarily low-income individuals and families.
MPC: Marathon Petroleum is the largest petroleum refining company in the United States, operating 13 refineries with a total crude oil processing capacity of approximately 3 million barrels per day. The company also operates the MPLX midstream partnership and Speedway-branded fuel stations.
MPWR: Monolithic Power Systems designs and manufactures high-performance analog and mixed-signal semiconductor products for power management. The company's chips are used in cloud computing, automotive, industrial, consumer electronics, and communications applications, with growing exposure to AI data center power delivery.
MRK: Merck & Co. is a global pharmaceutical company with leading franchises in oncology (Keytruda, the world's best-selling drug), vaccines (Gardasil HPV vaccine), animal health (Merck Animal Health), and hospital products. Keytruda is approved for over 30 cancer types and drives the majority of the company's growth.
MRNA: Moderna is a biotechnology company pioneering messenger RNA (mRNA) therapeutics and vaccines. The company gained global prominence for its COVID-19 vaccine (Spikevax) and is developing an extensive pipeline of mRNA-based vaccines and therapies for respiratory viruses, cancer, rare diseases, and autoimmune conditions.
MRSH: Marsh McLennan is the world's largest insurance broker and risk advisor, operating through Marsh (insurance brokerage), Guy Carpenter (reinsurance), Mercer (HR consulting), and Oliver Wyman (management consulting). The company helps organizations navigate complex risks and workforce challenges globally.
MRVL: Marvell Technology designs and sells semiconductor products for data infrastructure, including data center, enterprise networking, carrier infrastructure, consumer, and automotive/industrial markets. The company's custom silicon and electro-optics products are increasingly used in AI and cloud computing applications.
MS: Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing investment banking, securities, wealth management, and investment management services. The company serves corporations, governments, institutions, and individuals worldwide and manages over $6 trillion in client assets through its wealth management division.
MSCI: MSCI is a global provider of investment decision support tools including indexes, analytics, ESG ratings, and risk management solutions for institutional investors. The company's indexes, including the MSCI World and MSCI Emerging Markets, serve as benchmarks for trillions of dollars in investment assets.
MSFT: Microsoft is a global technology company and one of the world's most valuable corporations. Its products include the Windows operating system, Office 365 productivity suite, Azure cloud computing platform, LinkedIn professional network, and Xbox gaming platform. Microsoft is a leader in enterprise AI through its partnership with OpenAI.
MSI: Motorola Solutions is a global leader in public safety and enterprise communications, providing mission-critical two-way radios, body cameras, command center software, and video security systems. The company's products and services are used by law enforcement, fire departments, emergency medical services, and enterprises worldwide.
MTB: M&T Bank Corporation is a regional bank holding company providing commercial and retail banking services across the northeastern United States. The company focuses on middle-market commercial lending, commercial real estate, and consumer banking through its network of approximately 1,000 branches.
MTD: Mettler-Toledo International is a global manufacturer and marketer of precision instruments and services for laboratory, industrial, and food retailing applications. The company produces analytical balances, pipettes, thermal analysis systems, and automated chemistry instruments used in pharmaceutical, chemical, and food industries.
MU: Micron Technology is one of the world's largest memory and storage semiconductor companies, manufacturing DRAM, NAND flash, and NOR flash memory chips. The company's products are essential components in data centers, smartphones, PCs, automotive systems, and industrial applications, with growing demand from AI workloads.
NDAQ: Nasdaq Inc. operates global stock exchanges, including the Nasdaq Stock Market, and provides market technology, data, and analytics services. Beyond its exchange operations, the company sells anti-financial crime technology, surveillance solutions, and index licensing to financial institutions worldwide.
NEE: NextEra Energy is the world's largest generator of renewable energy from wind and solar, and operates Florida Power & Light, the largest electric utility in the United States by retail electricity produced and sold. The company is a leader in the clean energy transition through its NextEra Energy Resources subsidiary.
NEM: Newmont Corporation is the world's largest gold mining company, with operations and projects across North America, South America, Australia, and Africa. The company also produces copper, silver, zinc, and lead as byproducts of its gold mining operations.
NFLX: Netflix is the world's largest streaming entertainment service with over 260 million paid memberships across 190+ countries. The company produces and distributes original series, films, and documentaries, and has expanded into gaming and live events. Netflix pioneered the subscription video-on-demand model.
NI: NiSource is a regulated utility company providing natural gas and electric services to approximately 3.2 million customers across six states. The company operates through Columbia Gas (natural gas distribution in Ohio, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Kentucky, and Maryland) and NIPSCO (electric and gas service in northern Indiana).
NKE: NIKE is the world's largest athletic footwear and apparel company, designing and selling products for sports and fitness under the Nike, Jordan, and Converse brands. The company dominates global athletic footwear with iconic products including Air Force 1, Air Max, and Air Jordan.
NOC: Northrop Grumman is a leading global aerospace and defense company specializing in autonomous systems, space systems, cyber, and strike weapons. The company produces the B-21 Raider stealth bomber, Global Hawk drones, and James Webb Space Telescope, and is a major provider of space and missile defense systems.
NOW: ServiceNow is a leading enterprise software company providing cloud-based workflow automation and IT service management. The company's Now Platform enables organizations to digitize and automate workflows across IT, employee experience, customer service, and security operations.
NRG: NRG Energy is an integrated power company producing, selling, and delivering electricity and related products to customers across the United States. The company operates a diverse generation fleet and serves approximately 7.5 million residential and commercial customers through its retail brands including Vivint Smart Home.
NSC: Norfolk Southern is one of the largest Class I railroads in North America, operating approximately 19,300 miles of track across 22 eastern states. The railroad transports intermodal containers, coal, agriculture products, chemicals, metals, and automotive vehicles and parts.
NTAP: NetApp is a hybrid cloud data management and storage company providing data infrastructure solutions for enterprise and cloud environments. The company's products include all-flash storage arrays, cloud data services, and data management software used by organizations to manage, protect, and move their data.
NTRS: Northern Trust is a global financial services company providing wealth management, asset servicing, asset management, and banking solutions to corporations, institutions, affluent families, and individuals. The company is known for its custody and fund administration services for institutional investors.
NUE: Nucor Corporation is the largest steel producer in the United States, manufacturing steel products using electric arc furnace mini-mill technology. The company produces hot-rolled, cold-rolled, and galvanized sheet steel, steel bars, structural steel, and steel deck products for construction and manufacturing.
NVDA: NVIDIA Corporation is the world's leading designer of graphics processing units (GPUs) and AI computing platforms. The company's data center GPUs (A100, H100, H200, B100) dominate the AI training and inference market, and its products power gaming, professional visualization, automotive, and robotics applications.
NVR: NVR Inc. is one of the largest homebuilders in the United States, constructing and selling single-family detached homes, townhomes, and condominiums under the Ryan Homes, NVHomes, and Heartland Homes brands. The company operates primarily in the eastern United States.
NWS: News Corporation (Class B shares) is a global media and information services company operating across digital real estate (REA Group, Move/Realtor.com), news media (The Wall Street Journal, New York Post, The Times of London), book publishing (HarperCollins), and subscription video streaming (Foxtel).
NWSA: News Corporation (Class A shares) is a global media company controlled by the Murdoch family. Its key assets include Dow Jones (The Wall Street Journal, Barron's, MarketWatch), HarperCollins Publishers, digital real estate platforms (REA Group, Realtor.com), and news operations across the U.S., U.K., and Australia.
NXPI: NXP Semiconductors is a global semiconductor company specializing in secure connectivity and processing solutions for automotive, industrial, IoT, mobile, and communication infrastructure. The company is a leader in automotive semiconductors, providing chips for vehicle networking, radar, and electrification.
O: Realty Income Corporation is a REIT known as 'The Monthly Dividend Company,' owning over 13,000 commercial properties across the U.S. and Europe leased to tenants under long-term net lease agreements. The company's diversified portfolio includes retail, industrial, and gaming properties.
ODFL: Old Dominion Freight Line is a leading less-than-truckload (LTL) carrier in North America, providing regional, inter-regional, and national transportation services. The company is known for its superior on-time delivery performance and operates a network of over 250 service centers.
OKE: ONEOK is a leading midstream energy company owning one of the largest natural gas liquids (NGL) systems in the United States. The company gathers, processes, transports, and stores natural gas and NGLs, connecting prolific supply basins to key market centers.
OMC: Omnicom Group is a leading global marketing and corporate communications company, providing advertising, strategic media planning, digital marketing, public relations, and branding services. The company operates through a portfolio of agencies serving major brands worldwide.
ON: ON Semiconductor (onsemi) designs and manufactures intelligent power and sensing technologies for automotive, industrial, and cloud applications. The company is a leader in silicon carbide (SiC) power semiconductors for electric vehicles and provides image sensors for automotive advanced driver-assistance systems.
ORCL: Oracle Corporation is a global technology company providing database software, cloud infrastructure, and enterprise applications. The company's Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) competes with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud, and its enterprise software including database, ERP, HCM, and healthcare applications serve businesses worldwide.
ORLY: O'Reilly Automotive is a leading retailer of automotive aftermarket parts, tools, supplies, equipment, and accessories in the United States. The company operates over 6,000 stores and serves both do-it-yourself customers and professional mechanics through its extensive distribution network.
OTIS: Otis Worldwide is the world's largest elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. The company was spun off from United Technologies in 2020 and operates approximately 2.2 million customer units worldwide, providing new equipment, modernization, and maintenance services.
OXY: Occidental Petroleum is an international energy company engaged in oil and gas exploration and production, chemical manufacturing (OxyChem), and midstream and marketing operations. The company is also a leader in carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) technology through its 1PointFive subsidiary.
PANW: Palo Alto Networks is the world's largest cybersecurity company by revenue, providing next-generation firewalls, cloud security, endpoint protection, and security operations solutions. The company's platform approach integrates network security, cloud security, and AI-driven security operations for enterprises and governments.
PARA: Paramount Global is a mass media and entertainment company operating the CBS television network, Paramount Pictures film studio, cable networks (MTV, Nickelodeon, Comedy Central, BET), and the Paramount+ streaming service. The company produces and distributes content across television, film, and digital platforms.
PAYC: Paycom Software provides cloud-based human capital management (HCM) software for businesses, covering the full employment lifecycle including payroll, talent acquisition, time and labor management, HR management, and talent management. The company's single-database platform differentiates it from competitors.
PAYX: Paychex is a leading provider of payroll, human resource, and benefits outsourcing solutions for small and medium-sized businesses. The company serves approximately 740,000 clients across the United States and Europe, processing payroll for one in 12 private-sector employees in the U.S.
PCAR: PACCAR is a global technology company and manufacturer of premium commercial vehicles, including Kenworth, Peterbilt, and DAF trucks. The company also provides financial services, information technology, and distributes truck parts through its PACCAR Parts division.
PCG: PG&E Corporation is a utility holding company providing electric and natural gas services to approximately 16 million people across northern and central California through its subsidiary Pacific Gas and Electric Company. The company operates one of the largest combined natural gas and electric utilities in the United States.
PDD: PDD Holdings operates Pinduoduo, one of China's largest e-commerce platforms known for its group-buying model, and Temu, a fast-growing international e-commerce marketplace offering deeply discounted products. The company has rapidly expanded its global footprint through Temu's aggressive expansion.
PEAK: Healthpeak Properties is a REIT focused on life science, medical office, and continuing care retirement communities. The company owns properties in premier life science clusters and medical office buildings near major health systems across the United States.
PEG: Public Service Enterprise Group is a diversified energy company primarily operating through PSE&G, New Jersey's largest electric and gas utility serving approximately 2.3 million electric and 1.9 million gas customers. The company also has interests in nuclear generation and renewable energy.
PEP: PepsiCo is a global food and beverage company with a portfolio spanning beverages (Pepsi, Mountain Dew, Gatorade, Tropicana) and snacks (Lay's, Doritos, Cheetos, Tostitos, Quaker Oats). The company's complementary food and beverage portfolio generates approximately balanced revenue between its Frito-Lay snacks and beverage divisions.
PFE: Pfizer is a global pharmaceutical and biotechnology company known for developing and manufacturing medicines, vaccines, and consumer healthcare products. The company gained worldwide recognition for co-developing one of the first COVID-19 mRNA vaccines with BioNTech and has a broad portfolio spanning oncology, immunology, and rare diseases.
PFG: Principal Financial Group is a global financial services company offering retirement planning, asset management, insurance, and benefits solutions. The company serves businesses, institutions, and individuals worldwide, managing over $700 billion in assets across its investment management and retirement operations.
PG: Procter & Gamble is one of the world's largest consumer goods companies, producing and marketing a portfolio of trusted brands including Tide, Pampers, Gillette, Crest, Charmin, Bounty, and Downy. The company operates across five segments: beauty, grooming, healthcare, fabric care, and home care.
PGR: The Progressive Corporation is one of the largest auto insurance companies in the United States, known for its direct-to-consumer model, usage-based insurance (Snapshot), and competitive pricing through its comparison tool. The company also offers homeowners, renters, and commercial insurance.
PH: Parker-Hannifin is the global leader in motion and control technologies, manufacturing hydraulic, pneumatic, and electromechanical systems and components. The company's products are used in aerospace, industrial manufacturing, mobile equipment, and clean energy applications across virtually every industrial market.
PHM: PulteGroup is one of the largest homebuilders in the United States, constructing homes under the Pulte Homes, Del Webb, and Centex brands. The company operates in 42 markets across 23 states and offers homes ranging from first-time buyer to active adult communities.
PKG: Packaging Corporation of America is one of the largest producers of containerboard and corrugated packaging in the United States. The company operates a vertically integrated business, manufacturing linerboard and corrugating medium at its mills and converting them into corrugated containers at its box plants.
PLD: Prologis is the world's largest industrial REIT and logistics real estate company, owning and operating approximately 1.2 billion square feet of warehouse and distribution space across 19 countries. The company's properties serve the global supply chain for e-commerce, retail, and manufacturing tenants.
PLTR: Palantir Technologies builds software platforms for data integration and analysis used by government agencies and commercial enterprises. Its key products, Gotham (government) and Foundry (commercial), enable organizations to integrate, visualize, and act on complex data, with growing applications in AI and defense.
PM: Philip Morris International is the world's largest international tobacco company, selling cigarettes (Marlboro, L&M, Parliament) and smoke-free products in markets outside the United States. The company is transforming its business toward reduced-risk products, primarily its IQOS heated tobacco system.
PNC: The PNC Financial Services Group is one of the largest diversified financial services companies in the United States, providing retail banking, corporate banking, real estate finance, asset management, and residential mortgage banking across a national branch network.
POOL: Pool Corporation is the world's largest wholesale distributor of swimming pool supplies, equipment, and related leisure products. The company distributes more than 200,000 products to approximately 120,000 customers including pool builders, retail stores, and service companies across North America, Europe, and Australia.
PPG: PPG Industries is a global manufacturer of paints, coatings, and specialty materials. The company produces architectural and industrial coatings, automotive OEM and refinish paints, aerospace coatings, and performance materials used in construction, automotive, aerospace, and industrial applications.
PPL: PPL Corporation is a utility company providing electricity and natural gas to approximately 3.5 million customers in Pennsylvania, Kentucky, Virginia, and Rhode Island. The company focuses on regulated utility operations and is investing in grid modernization and renewable energy integration.
PRU: Prudential Financial is a global financial services company providing life insurance, annuities, retirement-related services, and investment management. The company serves individual and institutional customers worldwide through its PGIM investment management unit, which is one of the world's largest institutional asset managers.
PSA: Public Storage is the largest self-storage REIT in the United States, owning and operating approximately 2,900 self-storage facilities with over 200 million net rentable square feet. The company is the most recognizable brand in the self-storage industry.
PSX: Phillips 66 is a diversified energy manufacturing and logistics company operating petroleum refining, midstream, chemicals (through CPChem joint venture), and marketing and specialties businesses. The company operates 12 refineries with a total crude oil processing capacity of approximately 1.8 million barrels per day.
PTC: PTC provides industrial software solutions including computer-aided design (Creo), product lifecycle management (Windchill), IoT platforms (ThingWorx), and augmented reality (Vuforia). The company's software helps manufacturers digitally transform their product design, manufacturing, and service operations.
PWR: Quanta Services is the largest specialty contractor in North America, providing infrastructure services to the electric power, oil and gas, and telecommunications industries. The company designs, installs, and maintains the critical infrastructure that powers and connects communities.
PXD: Pioneer Natural Resources is a large independent oil and gas exploration and production company focused exclusively on the Permian Basin in West Texas. The company is one of the largest Permian Basin operators and was acquired by ExxonMobil in 2024.
PYPL: PayPal Holdings is a global digital payments company enabling digital and mobile payments for consumers and merchants. The company's platforms include PayPal, Venmo, Braintree, and Xoom, processing over $1.3 trillion in total payment volume annually across approximately 200 markets.
Q: Quintiles IMS Holdings, now known as IQVIA, is a leading provider of analytics, technology solutions, and clinical research services to the life sciences industry. The company combines data, analytics, and clinical trial expertise to help pharmaceutical companies develop and commercialize new therapies.
QCOM: Qualcomm Incorporated designs and licenses wireless technology and manufactures semiconductors, including the Snapdragon mobile platforms that power a majority of premium Android smartphones. The company also provides 5G, Wi-Fi, and automotive chips, and licenses its extensive portfolio of wireless technology patents.
RCL: Royal Caribbean Cruises is the world's second-largest cruise line operator, with brands including Royal Caribbean International, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea Cruises. The company operates a fleet of over 60 ships visiting approximately 1,000 destinations worldwide.
REG: Regency Centers is a REIT specializing in the ownership, operation, and development of grocery-anchored shopping centers in affluent and densely populated suburban trade areas. The company's portfolio includes approximately 400 properties across the United States.
REGN: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals is a biotechnology company that discovers, develops, and commercializes medicines for serious diseases. Key products include Eylea for eye diseases, Dupixent for allergic conditions, and Libtayo for cancer. The company is known for its proprietary VelociSuite drug development technology platform.
RF: Regions Financial Corporation is a bank holding company providing retail and commercial banking, wealth management, and mortgage services primarily in the southern and midwestern United States. The company operates approximately 1,300 banking offices across 15 states.
RJF: Raymond James Financial is a diversified financial services holding company providing investment banking, brokerage, asset management, banking, and cash management services. The company serves individuals, corporations, and municipalities through approximately 8,800 financial advisors across the United States, Canada, and the U.K.
RL: Ralph Lauren Corporation is a global leader in the design, marketing, and distribution of premium lifestyle products including apparel, footwear, accessories, home furnishings, and fragrances. The company operates under the Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren, and Lauren Ralph Lauren brands.
RMD: ResMed is a global leader in cloud-connected medical devices and digital health solutions for the treatment of sleep apnea, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, and other respiratory conditions. The company's products include CPAP machines, masks, ventilators, and software-as-a-service platforms.
ROK: Rockwell Automation is the world's largest company dedicated to industrial automation and digital transformation. The company provides hardware, software, and services for manufacturing operations, including programmable logic controllers, drives, sensors, and industrial IoT platforms.
ROL: Rollins Inc. is the parent company of Orkin and other pest control brands, providing pest and termite control services to residential and commercial customers across North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, and Australia. The company is one of the largest pest control companies in the world.
ROP: Roper Technologies is a diversified technology company operating application-specific software and technology-enabled products businesses. The company provides software for healthcare, transportation, food and water, and financial and compliance markets through a portfolio of niche market-leading businesses.
ROST: Ross Stores operates Ross Dress for Less and dd's DISCOUNTS, off-price retail chains offering brand-name and designer merchandise at significant discounts. The company operates approximately 2,100 stores across the United States and is the second-largest off-price retailer behind TJX Companies.
RSG: Republic Services is one of the largest waste management and environmental services companies in the United States, providing non-hazardous solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal services. The company serves approximately 14 million customers across 41 states.
RTX: RTX Corporation (formerly Raytheon Technologies) is a major aerospace and defense company operating through Collins Aerospace (avionics, interiors, mechanical systems), Pratt & Whitney (jet engines), and Raytheon (missiles, defense systems). The company serves both commercial aerospace and military defense markets globally.
RVTY: Revvity (formerly PerkinElmer) is a global life sciences and diagnostics company providing instruments, reagents, software, and services for health sciences research, clinical diagnostics, and applied genomics. The company focuses on advancing human health through early detection and precision medicine.
SBAC: SBA Communications is a REIT that owns and operates wireless communications infrastructure, including over 39,000 cell towers and small cell networks primarily in the Americas. The company leases antenna space on its towers to wireless carriers and broadband providers.
SBUX: Starbucks Corporation is the world's largest coffeehouse chain, operating over 35,000 stores across 80+ markets. The company sells specialty coffee drinks, food items, and packaged goods, and has built a powerful loyalty program and mobile ordering platform. Starbucks is synonymous with premium coffee culture globally.
SCHW: Charles Schwab Corporation is one of the largest brokerage and wealth management firms in the United States, providing investment services, trading, banking, and financial advisory to individual investors and registered investment advisors. The company pioneered discount brokerage and commission-free trading.
SEDG: SolarEdge Technologies designs and manufactures smart energy solutions for solar photovoltaic systems, including DC-optimized inverters, power optimizers, and energy storage solutions. The company's products maximize energy production at the individual solar panel level for residential and commercial installations.
SHW: The Sherwin-Williams Company is the world's largest paint and coatings manufacturer, operating over 4,700 company-owned stores and selling products to professional painters, contractors, and consumers. Its brands include Sherwin-Williams, Valspar, HGTV HOME, and Minwax.
SJM: The J.M. Smucker Company manufactures and markets branded food and beverage products including Smucker's jams, JIF peanut butter, Folgers coffee, Uncrustables sandwiches, and Milk-Bone dog treats. The company focuses on leading brands in coffee, peanut butter, pet food, and frozen handheld categories.
SLB: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is the world's largest oilfield services company, providing technology, integrated project management, and information solutions to the oil and gas industry. The company operates in over 100 countries and leads in digital transformation of the energy industry.
SMCI: Super Micro Computer designs and manufactures high-performance server solutions for data centers, cloud computing, and AI workloads. The company offers a complete line of server and storage systems, including GPU-optimized platforms that have seen surging demand from AI infrastructure buildouts.
SNA: Snap-on Incorporated manufactures and markets high-quality tools, equipment, diagnostics, and repair information for professional technicians in the automotive, aviation, marine, and other industries. The company sells primarily through its iconic Snap-on mobile tool distribution vans and direct-to-industrial channels.
SNDK: SanDisk is a global leader in flash memory storage solutions, including SSDs, memory cards, and USB flash drives for consumers, enterprises, and data centers. The company was historically independent and is now part of the Western Digital family of brands, recently re-listed as a standalone entity.
SNPS: Synopsys is a leading provider of electronic design automation (EDA) software, semiconductor IP, and software security solutions. The company's tools are used by chip designers to create complex integrated circuits, and its IP portfolio accelerates chip development for leading semiconductor companies.
SO: The Southern Company is one of the largest utilities in the United States, providing electric and natural gas service to approximately 9 million customers across the southeastern states through its subsidiaries Georgia Power, Alabama Power, Mississippi Power, and Southern Company Gas.
SPG: Simon Property Group is the largest shopping mall REIT in the world, owning and operating premier shopping, dining, and entertainment destinations including malls, Premium Outlets, and The Mills across North America, Europe, and Asia.
SPGI: S&P Global is a provider of financial market intelligence, credit ratings (S&P Ratings), and indices (S&P 500, Dow Jones). The company merged with IHS Markit and provides essential data, analytics, and benchmarks to capital and commodity markets worldwide.
SRE: Sempra is a North American energy infrastructure company operating regulated utilities (Southern California Gas, San Diego Gas & Electric) and infrastructure businesses in renewables, LNG, and energy networks. The company serves approximately 40 million consumers across the Americas.
STE: STERIS is a global provider of infection prevention, contamination control, surgical products, and gastrointestinal endoscopy equipment. The company serves hospitals, pharmaceutical manufacturers, and research laboratories with sterilization systems, consumables, and services for maintaining sterile processing environments.
STLD: Steel Dynamics is one of the largest steel producers and metals recyclers in the United States, using electric arc furnace technology to produce steel from recycled scrap. The company manufactures flat-rolled, structural, rail, and specialty steel products for construction, automotive, and manufacturing.
STT: State Street Corporation is a leading provider of financial services to institutional investors, including investment servicing, investment management, and research and trading. The company custodies over $40 trillion in assets and manages SPDR ETFs, including the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), one of the world's largest ETFs.
STX: Seagate Technology is a leading manufacturer of data storage solutions, including hard disk drives (HDDs) and solid-state drives (SSDs) for enterprise data centers, cloud computing, and consumer markets. The company is one of the two largest HDD manufacturers globally alongside Western Digital.
STZ: Constellation Brands is a leading producer and marketer of beer, wine, and spirits, holding the exclusive rights to import and market Corona, Modelo, and Pacifico beer brands in the United States. The company's beer business is the fastest-growing major beer supplier in the U.S. market.
SW: Smurfit Westrock is a global leader in sustainable paper and packaging solutions, formed from the combination of Smurfit Kappa and WestRock. The company produces corrugated packaging, containerboard, and other paper-based products for the consumer goods, e-commerce, industrial, and food service industries.
SWKS: Skyworks Solutions designs and manufactures semiconductors for mobile communications, automotive, broadband, industrial, and IoT applications. The company's analog and mixed-signal chips enable wireless connectivity in smartphones, connected cars, smart homes, and industrial IoT devices.
SYF: Synchrony Financial is a consumer financial services company providing private label credit cards, dual co-branded cards, general purpose co-branded cards, and consumer installment loans. The company partners with major retailers, healthcare providers, and digital platforms to offer promotional financing.
SYK: Stryker Corporation is one of the world's leading medical technology companies, manufacturing orthopedic implants (joint replacements, trauma products), surgical instruments, neurotechnology products, and hospital beds and stretchers. The company's Mako robotic-arm assisted surgery system is a market leader.
SYY: Sysco Corporation is the world's largest foodservice distributor, delivering food products, kitchen equipment, and smallwares to restaurants, hospitals, schools, hotels, and other institutional customers. The company operates approximately 340 distribution facilities globally and serves about 700,000 customer locations.
T: AT&T is one of the world's largest telecommunications companies, providing wireless services, broadband, and business solutions. The company operates the largest wireless network in the United States serving over 70 million postpaid phone subscribers, and provides fiber broadband to millions of homes and businesses.
TAP: Molson Coors Beverage Company is a global brewing company producing and marketing well-known beer brands including Coors Light, Miller Lite, Molson Canadian, Blue Moon, and Peroni Nastro Azzurro. The company is the second-largest brewer in the Americas by volume.
TDG: TransDigm Group is a global aerospace manufacturer and supplier of highly engineered aircraft components. The company produces proprietary, sole-source products including actuators, ignition systems, pumps, valves, and other critical components used in commercial and military aircraft worldwide.
TDY: Teledyne Technologies is a provider of sophisticated instrumentation, digital imaging products, aerospace and defense electronics, and engineered systems. The company's products are used in deep ocean exploration, environmental monitoring, air and water quality analysis, factory automation, and space science.
TEAM: Atlassian Corporation develops collaboration and productivity software for software development and project management teams. Its products include Jira (project tracking), Confluence (team collaboration), Trello (task management), and Bitbucket (code hosting), serving over 260,000 customers worldwide.
TECH: Bio-Techne develops and manufactures proteins, antibodies, and diagnostic tools for the life sciences and clinical diagnostics markets. The company's products are used in research for cancer, immunology, neuroscience, and stem cell biology, as well as in clinical diagnostic testing.
TEL: TE Connectivity is a global industrial technology leader manufacturing connectors, sensors, and electronic components for the automotive, industrial, aerospace, defense, medical, and communications markets. The company's products enable connectivity in harsh environments across virtually every major industry.
TER: Teradyne is a leading supplier of automatic test equipment (ATE) for testing semiconductors, electronic systems, and wireless products. The company also provides industrial automation solutions through its Universal Robots collaborative robot (cobot) business and mobile robots for warehouse logistics.
TFC: Truist Financial Corporation is a bank holding company formed from the merger of BB&T and SunTrust Banks, operating as the seventh-largest commercial bank in the United States. The company provides banking, insurance, and investment services through its network of branches across the southeastern and mid-Atlantic states.
TGT: Target Corporation is one of the largest general merchandise retailers in the United States, operating approximately 1,950 stores. The company sells apparel, home goods, beauty products, electronics, food, and essentials, and has built a successful portfolio of owned brands including Goodfellow, Cat & Jack, and Threshold.
TJX: The TJX Companies is the world's largest off-price retailer, operating T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods, Sierra, and Homesense stores. The company offers brand-name and designer merchandise at prices generally 20-60% below department store prices, with over 4,900 stores across nine countries.
TMO: Thermo Fisher Scientific is the world's largest life sciences tools company, providing analytical instruments, reagents, consumables, software, and services to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, academic, clinical, and government customers. The company enables drug discovery, diagnostics development, and scientific research worldwide.
TMUS: T-Mobile US is the second-largest wireless carrier in the United States, serving over 100 million customers. The company merged with Sprint in 2020 and has since led the industry in 5G network deployment, customer growth, and subscriber satisfaction through its Un-carrier strategy.
TPL: Texas Pacific Land Corporation is one of the largest landowners in the state of Texas, owning approximately 880,000 acres of land primarily in the Permian Basin. The company generates revenue from oil and gas royalties, water services, land sales, and surface leases, benefiting from the prolific Permian Basin energy development.
TPR: Tapestry is a global house of modern luxury lifestyle brands including Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman. The company designs and markets accessories, handbags, footwear, and ready-to-wear collections through retail stores, e-commerce, and wholesale channels worldwide.
TRGP: Targa Resources is a midstream natural gas and natural gas liquids (NGL) company operating gathering, processing, transportation, and logistics assets primarily in the Permian Basin, the Midcontinent, and the Gulf Coast. The company connects producers to downstream markets.
TROW: T. Rowe Price Group is a global investment management firm providing a broad range of mutual funds, advisory services, and retirement plan solutions. The company is known for its active management approach and manages approximately $1.5 trillion in assets for individual and institutional investors.
TRV: The Travelers Companies is one of the largest property casualty insurance companies in the United States, providing commercial and personal property and casualty insurance products. The company is a Dow Jones Industrial Average component and is known for its disciplined underwriting approach.
TSCO: Tractor Supply Company is the largest rural lifestyle retail chain in the United States, operating over 2,200 stores. The company sells products for farming, ranching, animal care, gardening, and outdoor living, serving recreational farmers, ranchers, and rural homeowners.
TSLA: Tesla is the world's most valuable automaker and a leader in electric vehicles, energy storage, and solar energy. The company produces the Model S, 3, X, Y, and Cybertruck, operates a global Supercharger network, and manufactures battery energy storage systems. Tesla's CEO Elon Musk has positioned the company at the forefront of autonomous driving and AI.
TSN: Tyson Foods is one of the world's largest food companies, producing and marketing chicken, beef, pork, and prepared foods. The company operates through its Beef, Pork, Chicken, and Prepared Foods segments and sells products under the Tyson, Jimmy Dean, Hillshire Farm, Ball Park, and Sara Lee brands.
TT: Trane Technologies is a global climate technology company providing heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) solutions for buildings, homes, and transportation. The company operates through its Trane and Thermo King brands, focusing on energy-efficient and sustainable climate control.
TTD: The Trade Desk is an independent demand-side platform (DSP) enabling advertisers to purchase digital advertising across channels including connected TV, display, mobile, video, audio, and social media. The company processes over 13 million ad impressions per second.
TTWO: Take-Two Interactive Software is a leading developer and publisher of video games, known for Grand Theft Auto (Rockstar Games), NBA 2K and WWE 2K (2K Games), and Zynga mobile games. The Grand Theft Auto franchise is one of the best-selling entertainment properties in history.
TXN: Texas Instruments is one of the world's largest semiconductor companies, specializing in analog chips and embedded processors. The company's products convert real-world signals into digital data and are used in industrial, automotive, personal electronics, communications, and enterprise applications.
UAL: United Airlines Holdings is one of the world's largest airlines, serving approximately 200 million passengers annually across a global route network spanning six continents. The company operates major hubs in Chicago, Denver, Houston, Los Angeles, Newark, San Francisco, and Washington Dulles.
UBER: Uber Technologies operates a global platform connecting riders with drivers (Uber, UberX, Uber Black), food delivery customers with restaurants (Uber Eats), and shippers with carriers (Uber Freight). The company operates in over 70 countries and has expanded into grocery delivery, alcohol delivery, and advertising.
UDR: UDR Inc. is a REIT that owns, operates, acquires, and develops apartment communities across the United States. The company's portfolio includes approximately 60,000 apartment homes in markets including Washington D.C., San Francisco, Boston, Nashville, and Denver.
ULTA: Ulta Beauty is the largest beauty specialty retailer in the United States, offering cosmetics, skincare, fragrance, haircare, and salon services. The company operates approximately 1,400 stores and an e-commerce platform, carrying over 25,000 products from approximately 600 brands.
UNH: UnitedHealth Group is the largest healthcare company in the world by revenue, operating through UnitedHealthcare (health insurance serving over 50 million members) and Optum (health services including pharmacy benefits, care delivery, and data analytics). The company is a Dow Jones Industrial Average component.
UNP: Union Pacific is one of the largest Class I railroads in North America, operating approximately 32,000 miles of track across 23 western states. The railroad transports coal, industrial products, intermodal containers, agricultural commodities, and automotive vehicles across the western two-thirds of the United States.
UPS: United Parcel Service is the world's largest package delivery company and a global leader in supply chain management and freight transportation. The company delivers approximately 25 million packages and documents daily across more than 220 countries through its integrated air and ground network.
URI: United Rentals is the world's largest equipment rental company, with a fleet of approximately 1,000,000 units of construction, industrial, and homeowner equipment. The company operates over 1,500 branches across North America, Europe, and Australia, serving construction, industrial, and infrastructure markets.
USB: U.S. Bancorp is the parent company of U.S. Bank National Association, the fifth-largest bank in the United States. The company provides banking, investment, mortgage, trust, and payment services to individuals, businesses, and institutions primarily across the western and midwestern United States.
V: Visa is the world's largest digital payments network, processing more transactions than any other payment network globally. The company connects consumers, businesses, banks, and governments across more than 200 countries, facilitating electronic fund transfers through Visa-branded credit, debit, and prepaid cards.
VICI: VICI Properties is a REIT specializing in experiential properties, primarily owning gaming, hospitality, and entertainment destinations. The company's portfolio includes iconic Las Vegas Strip properties such as Caesars Palace, MGM Grand, and The Venetian, as well as regional gaming properties across the United States.
VLO: Valero Energy is the world's largest independent petroleum refiner, operating 15 refineries with a combined throughput capacity of approximately 3.2 million barrels per day. The company is also a major producer of renewable diesel and ethanol through its renewable fuels operations.
VLTO: Veralto Corporation is a water analytics, treatment, and product quality company spun off from Danaher in 2023. The company provides water quality testing instruments, chemical treatment solutions, and product quality and innovation solutions for the print and packaging, color science, and product identification markets.
VMC: Vulcan Materials is the largest producer of construction aggregates (crushed stone, sand, gravel) in the United States. The company's products are used in virtually all forms of construction including highways, buildings, airports, and water infrastructure, making it a key beneficiary of infrastructure spending.
VRNS: Varonis Systems provides data security and analytics software that protects enterprise data from insider threats and cyberattacks. The company's platform provides visibility and control over unstructured data across on-premises and cloud environments, helping organizations detect threats and maintain compliance.
VRSK: Verisk Analytics provides data analytics and risk assessment solutions primarily to the insurance industry, as well as energy and specialized markets. The company's products help insurers price policies, assess risk, detect fraud, and manage catastrophe exposure using proprietary data and advanced analytics.
VRSN: VeriSign operates critical internet infrastructure including the authoritative registry for .com and .net domain names and two of the 13 internet root name servers. The company ensures the stability and security of the domain name system (DNS) that enables internet navigation for billions of users worldwide.
VRTX: Vertex Pharmaceuticals is a biotechnology company that has transformed the treatment of cystic fibrosis (CF) with its portfolio of CF medicines, including Trikafta, which treats approximately 90% of CF patients. The company is expanding into pain management, kidney disease, and gene editing therapies.
VST: Vistra Corp. is an integrated retail electricity and power generation company operating in major competitive wholesale and retail electricity markets across the United States. The company owns and operates a diverse fleet including natural gas, nuclear, coal, and solar generation facilities.
VTR: Ventas is a REIT focused on healthcare real estate, owning a diversified portfolio of senior housing, research and innovation centers, medical office buildings, and healthcare facilities. The company partners with leading health systems, research institutions, and senior living operators.
VZ: Verizon Communications is one of the largest telecommunications companies in the world, providing wireless services to over 100 million connections, broadband and fiber-optic internet (Fios), and business communications solutions. The company operates the largest 4G LTE network in the United States and is expanding its 5G coverage.
WAB: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies (Wabtec) is a global provider of equipment, systems, and services for the transit and freight rail industries. The company manufactures locomotives, braking systems, signaling equipment, and digital mining solutions, serving railroads and transit operators worldwide.
WAT: Waters Corporation designs and manufactures analytical instruments including liquid chromatography, mass spectrometry, and thermal analysis systems. The company's instruments are used in pharmaceutical development, quality control, environmental testing, and food safety analysis worldwide.
WBD: Warner Bros. Discovery is a global media and entertainment company formed from the merger of WarnerMedia and Discovery. The company operates Warner Bros. film and TV studio, HBO, CNN, Discovery Channel, TBS, TNT, and the Max streaming platform.
WDAY: Workday provides enterprise cloud applications for financial management and human capital management. The company's software-as-a-service platform helps organizations manage their workforce, payroll, talent, planning, and financial operations, serving large enterprises and educational institutions.
WDC: Western Digital is a data infrastructure company providing storage devices and platforms for data centers, cloud computing, and consumer markets. The company manufactures hard disk drives (HDDs) and flash-based solid-state drives (SSDs), serving enterprise, embedded, and consumer applications.
WEC: WEC Energy Group is a regulated utility holding company providing electricity and natural gas to approximately 4.6 million customers in Wisconsin, Illinois, Michigan, and Minnesota. The company is investing in renewable energy and infrastructure modernization.
WELL: Welltower is the largest healthcare REIT in the United States, owning a portfolio of senior housing, post-acute care, and outpatient medical properties. The company partners with leading senior living operators and health systems to deliver care and housing for an aging population.
WFC: Wells Fargo is one of the four largest banks in the United States, providing banking, mortgage, consumer finance, and commercial financial services. The company serves one in three U.S. households and operates a nationwide branch network, with particular strength in mortgage lending and wealth management.
WHR: Whirlpool Corporation is one of the world's largest home appliance manufacturers, producing washing machines, dryers, refrigerators, dishwashers, ovens, and other appliances under the Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, and Amana brands across North America, Latin America, Europe, and Asia.
WM: Waste Management is the largest waste management company in North America, providing waste collection, transfer, recycling, resource recovery, and disposal services. The company operates the largest network of landfills, transfer stations, and recycling facilities in the United States and Canada.
WMB: The Williams Companies is a large-scale natural gas processing and transportation company, operating the Transco pipeline system, the largest natural gas transmission pipeline in the United States by volume. The company connects natural gas supply basins to growing demand markets along the eastern seaboard.
WMT: Walmart is the world's largest company by revenue and the largest retailer globally, operating over 10,500 stores and clubs across 19 countries. The company serves approximately 240 million customers weekly through its Walmart U.S., Walmart International, and Sam's Club divisions, and has a rapidly growing e-commerce business.
WRB: W. R. Berkley Corporation is a commercial lines property casualty insurance holding company operating across two segments: Insurance (commercial insurance) and Reinsurance. The company focuses on specialty lines of business through over 50 operating units worldwide.
WSM: Williams-Sonoma is a specialty retailer of high-quality home products, operating under the Williams Sonoma, Pottery Barn, Pottery Barn Kids, Pottery Barn Teen, West Elm, and Rejuvenation brands. The company operates stores and a significant e-commerce business, with digital sales representing a majority of revenue.
WST: West Pharmaceutical Services designs and manufactures packaging components and delivery systems for injectable drugs and healthcare products. The company produces rubber stoppers, syringe components, metal seals, and advanced drug delivery systems used by pharmaceutical and biotech companies worldwide.
WTW: Willis Towers Watson is a global advisory, broking, and solutions company providing risk management, insurance brokerage, employee benefits, and organizational consulting services. The company helps clients manage people, risk, and capital through its Human Capital, Corporate Risk, and Investment management divisions.
WY: Weyerhaeuser Company is one of the world's largest private owners of timberlands, managing approximately 11 million acres of timberlands in the United States. The company also operates wood products manufacturing businesses producing lumber, engineered wood products, and other building materials.
WYNN: Wynn Resorts is a developer and operator of luxury casino resorts, with properties including Wynn Las Vegas, Encore Las Vegas, Wynn Macau, and Wynn Palace in Macau. The company's resorts are known for their premium guest experiences, fine dining, retail, and entertainment offerings.
XEL: Xcel Energy is a regulated utility company providing electricity and natural gas to approximately 3.7 million customers across eight states in the Upper Midwest and Southwest. The company is a leader among U.S. utilities in wind energy production and has committed to carbon-free electricity by 2050.
XOM: Exxon Mobil is the world's largest publicly traded international oil and gas company, operating across the upstream (exploration and production), downstream (refining and marketing), and chemical segments. The company is a major producer of crude oil and natural gas and one of the largest refiners globally.
XYL: Xylem is a global water technology company providing solutions for water and wastewater utilities, industrial, and commercial building services. The company manufactures pumps, treatment systems, analytics instruments, and smart metering solutions that help customers transport, treat, test, and efficiently use water.
XYZ: Block (formerly Square) is a financial technology company providing commerce tools and financial services for businesses and individuals. Its ecosystem includes Square (merchant point-of-sale, payments, and business tools), Cash App (peer-to-peer payments, banking, and investing), and TIDAL (music streaming).
YUM: Yum! Brands is one of the world's largest restaurant companies, operating and franchising KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut, and The Habit Burger Grill brands. The company has over 55,000 restaurants in more than 155 countries, with the majority operated by independent franchisees.
ZBH: Zimmer Biomet Holdings is a global medical technology company specializing in musculoskeletal healthcare. The company designs and manufactures orthopedic reconstructive implants (knee and hip replacements), sports medicine products, spine implants, dental implants, and robotic-assisted surgical technology (ROSA).
ZBRA: Zebra Technologies provides enterprise asset intelligence solutions including barcode scanners, mobile computers, RFID readers, and real-time location systems. The company's products help businesses track and manage assets, inventory, and people across supply chain, retail, healthcare, and manufacturing environments.
ZS: Zscaler operates a cloud-native security platform that enables secure digital transformation by providing zero trust network access, secure web gateway, and cloud workload protection. The company's Zero Trust Exchange processes over 300 billion daily transactions, securing users, devices, and applications.
ZTS: Zoetis is the world's largest animal health company, developing and manufacturing medicines, vaccines, diagnostics, and technologies for companion animals and livestock. The company's products address diseases and conditions in dogs, cats, cattle, pigs, poultry, and other animals across more than 100 countries.
